programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedy quiz mash-up. Bob Mortimer and Tom Allen take on Richard Ayoade and Harriet Dyer. With Ian Smith and Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Matt and Lorraine want to create a new front entrance to their quirky country home, but uncover problems above and below ground.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Laura, Diane and Huey prepare for a ceremony at their Groveland wedding venue, but heat and horses cause problems. Singer Sarah turns her Orlando living room into a cabaret club.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon and Shaun Garner are channelling a higher power, hoping to lead reiki master Cathy to her ideal property. With a top budget of £140,000, the Garners hunt with Cathy for a second home for visiting friends and family in the sun.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Denise and best pal Karen have enlisted the help of Jonnie Irwin to find the perfect holiday home for Denise and her beloved Jack Russell around Guardamar del Segura on the Costa Blanca.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Annette and Mark dream of finding the perfect holiday home with a pool on Spain's Orihuela Costa for £150k. Jasmine Harman's on hand to make sure the search goes swimmingly.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Steve and Jackie want a holiday home in Guardamar del Segura, southeast Spain, to enjoy with their family and friends. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their budget of £80k can buy.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Near Cognac, Bart and Sarah rush to finish a bathroom for their best friends' wedding. And sustainability champion Trish is planning a Pyrenean palace for rescued battery hens.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mark and Loretta look for their dream holiday home near Estepona on Spain's Costa del Sol, for their budget of £200,000. Can Craig Rowe find them the perfect place?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Aberdeen is offshore logistics supervisor Michelle, who hopes to win over her guests with her `Weegie' wit and prove that Glaswegians have more fun.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Confident economics undergraduate David hosts on night two in Aberdeen. His 1920s Gatsby-themed dress code goes down a storm, but does his student grub impress?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, oil and gas purchasing specialist Sylvian hosts. Sylvian is hoping her attention to detail and an ambitious global-themed menu will be enough to impress her guests.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, host Naz is, by his own admission, not the finest cook. Naz has to placate his guests, nearly all of whom he has managed to offend over the week.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Aberdeen, journalist Turid hopes her home-grown produce will impress. With farmyard antics, plenty of laughter, tears and surprises, there's all to play for.

programme.title: Perfect Pub Walks with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Chris McCausland joins Xander on three monumental Monmouthshire meanders, and chats about his family, career and expectations of others.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: When it comes to dating etiquette, Breeze the American bulldog cross leaves a lot to be desired when he meets 31-year-old Charlotte. Will she still fall for him though?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Jeremy and Patricia are rushed to Queen's Medical Centre, Nottingham with multiple injuries after a high-speed car crash. And Andrew has fallen three meters from a ladder at work.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Air ambulances bring the emergency room to critically ill or injured people across the country, including a man in cardiac arrest and a woman trampled by a foal.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Miquita and Andi Oliver, Mo Gilligan, Jamie Carragher, Nick Grimshaw, Clare Balding, Shaun Ryder, Bez and loads more celebs hit the sofa for the best TV this week.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will there be chemistry between two self-confessed nerds when engineer Ellie meets nuclear project manager Callum? Author Beckie meets poet Paul. And disco DJ John's love life's been stuck on pause...

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: World's Biggest Curry Restaurant
programme.desc: What does it take to run the largest Pakistani Indian restaurant on earth? We go behind the scenes of the Royal Nawaab in Greater Manchester to see how they deliver perfection at scale.

programme.title: World's Biggest Curry Restaurant
programme.desc: What does it take to run the largest Pakistani Indian restaurant on earth? We go behind the scenes of the Royal Nawaab in Greater Manchester to see how they deliver perfection at scale.

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke create a tasty tipple from foraged seaside ingredients. And in Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp.

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: A definitive one-stop shop for all things cruise, bringing you the hottest destinations and the latest deals to ensure that your next cruise ship holiday is your greatest one yet.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police interceptors tackle a difficult night shift. Ortis is with Spike as they pursue a fleeing car that drives into a dead end, while Rick is with dog handler Ian as they're called to back up the firearms squad.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors take on youngsters who cause carnage on our roads and put themselves and others in danger. Spike and dog handler Ian pursue a 20-year-old drunk driver into a traveller's site. Ben goes head-to-head with a speeding boy racer.

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly
programme.desc: A cattle drover, en route to selling his herd to the Yankees, is kidnapped by a band of ruthless Confederate troopers who plan to drive the herd into their own territory. The situation is inflamed when the cattleman helps the rebel leader's lover to flee from him.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly
programme.desc: A cattle drover, en route to selling his herd to the Yankees, is kidnapped by a band of ruthless Confederate troopers who plan to drive the herd into their own territory. The situation is inflamed when the cattleman helps the rebel leader's lover to flee from him.

programme.title: Lawman
programme.desc: Frontier tale about an impassive, out-of-town marshal's encounter with the hostile community of Sabbath. When he chases a group of wanted men into the town, the lawman finds that the local sheriff and much of the populace are in the pay of a criminal rancher, and he soon finds himself confronting difficult opposition as he tries to carry out his business.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A burnt out van abandoned on an isolated towpath presents a serious challenge for Steve and Marta, but the solution comes in the form of a bespoke, bright yellow rescue truck. A lorry loaded with tarmac breaks down spurring a race against time.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Drink drivers are caught in the act as they try to beat the system.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Eurocopter gets down and dirty in a lengthy manhunt, a vanload of villains lose control in a high speed pursuit and a drunk driver crushes his Corsa like it is a tin can. Elsewhere, a speed freak thinks he can outrun the law, there is aggravation and abuse on Derbyshire's mean streets and the interceptors facing off against an eight legged enemy.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A horsebox carries an illegal load, one of the team's top cars becomes a victim of a drink-driver and a road user gets a lesson in how to transport children safely.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A van is found to contain a high value counterfeit cargo, a crash leaves a female passenger trapped in a car, and a tractor is used to pursue a suspect.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: A police helicopter is sent to the scene after a car stolen in Gloucester is crashed by a pair of fleeing youths. Meanwhile, a night worker in a petrol station is attacked by a customer.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: When caught with an open container in Florida, a woman lies about her age. Officers ask a group of spring breakers to fill in a huge hole they dug on the beach. A man dressed like Rocky is stopped.

programme.title: Cops
programme.sub-title: Spring Break: Bombed at the Beach
programme.desc: Test kits for illicit substances are handed out to beachgoers. K-9 'Delta' conducts bomb detection drills on the sand. A man asks Deputies to pose for a picture with his summons for underage drinking.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: When a lonely woman on a blind date is brutally murdered, Mulder links her case to earlier disappearances of women looking for love in the wrong places. Scully's analysis reveals strange discrepancies in the victim's weight and body composition - somehow the killer removed all the fat from the corpse - and he is still out there looking for another victim.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Zenica prison is the toughest prison in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Discipline and fitness are essentials that the prison administration regularly reviews.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: As John's retirement looms, he shares one last heavy haul with his son Justin. Meanwhile, Sam competes with traffic to pick up a load of racing livestock.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A coach that has driven off the road into a hedgerow needs to be rescued before rush hour traffic gets disrupted. A dustbin lorry has gotten stuck in the mud on a narrow country road.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In a muddy Leicestershire field, recovery operator Simon is confronted with not one, but two trucks in need of rescuing. Lantern recovery operator Merrie is on her way to urgently assist a stricken unit and trailer that's broken down.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team tracks down a driver who has legged it from the cops. Elsewhere, Ange stands up to a couple of heavyweights, Phil helps hatch a plan to apprehend a disqualified driver, and Craig is unimpressed by one man's feeble attempt to disguise his identity.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dangerous drug dealer lets far more than the cat out of the bag, name swapping friends leave the interceptors baffled, and Phil has a shock for an illegal driver while Mac deals with a drunk drummer.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mac stops a driver for having no insurance and unearths a huge drugs haul and the chopper tracks down two notorious burglars. Elsewhere, Tim and Ange stop an alleged road-rage suspect armed with a table leg, and Phil wakes up the neighbours in his search for a couple of taxi-fare dodgers.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Concerned 999 callers report an HGV swerving all over the M6. PCs Jamie Barlow and Amy Hollis team up to search for the vehicle in question. When they finally catch up with the driver, they discover the awful truth behind his dangerous driving.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Andy Doran is chasing a gang of thieves fleeing a shop robbery in Ellesmere Port. And PC Chris Swash races to the scene of a head-on car collision that has left a driver trapped.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Ian Cosgrove tries to pull over a suspected drink driver refusing to stop. Meanwhile, in Warrington, PC Chris Swash is blue-lighting to a collision involving multiple vehicles. As he makes his way to the location, another collision takes place.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Matt Turner chases down a drink driver who's caused a collision on the morning school run, and on the M6 a broken down BMW stinks of cannabis.

programme.title: The First of the Few
programme.desc: Absorbing film biography of R J Mitchell, the designer of the Spitfire, the aircraft that enabled the Royal Air Force to defeat the might of Germany's Luftwaffe during the Battle of Britain.

programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.desc: Novice bar owners Dan and Charlotte host a fish-and-quiz Friday theme night, but having only ever cooked at home, the pressure is on.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: We revisit the Costas' only transgender rock'n'roll singer Rocking Stephanie who has added a new string to her tribute act bow by transforming into Elvis.

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: At Vauxhall station, one the capital's fare-dodging ultra-hot spots, a team of six revenue protection officers set a trap to block rush hour fare dodgers.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dave is on the lookout for a car driving dangerously, when suddenly it pulls out in front of him. John's 100mph blue-light run panics an unrelated driver into a crash. Veteran cop Jason's `copper's instinct' leads him to suspect a motorcyclist.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Violent assaults against police have leapt up by a third in just 4 years - meaning that 30 officers are seriously injured in the line of duty every day.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Craig responds to a report of an incident involving a car on fire with the driver trapped inside. In Derby, as people make their way home from a boozy night out, Alex is on the lookout for drink drivers chancing their luck.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors catch a gang of car thieves red-handed. Elsewhere, officers clamp down on car crime at Essex's biggest shopping centre and help get the M25 moving after an accident.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A massive manhunt ensues after three suspects flee onto a farm, the team raids a drugs den and officers stumble across a suspicious-looking postman.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A knife-wielding man ruins New Year celebrations and the interceptors race to capture a gang of fugitives. Elsewhere, snow causes chaos on the roads and Grant becomes furious with the guardian of a child.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A serial fraudster with expensive tastes ends up behind bars, while a damaged sofa claim and a prisoner's injury story fall apart when cameras and insurers reveal the truth. (S2 Ep15)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf and Brax are fishing. Alf questions his distracted mood, and Brax opens up about his dilemma - whether to stay in the Bay, raise his son here, or return to WA.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi fantasises to Mackenzie about what the early pregnancy kicks could mean for the baby's future - maybe they're about to raise a future Olympian.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Jane faces staffing shortages as emergencies arrive, including Harry with sepsis, Ian with an eye injury and Isaac with a stone in his ear.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: A young man arrives in excruciating pain from what may be a dangerous pulmonary embolism, staff rush to help a 66-year-old patient in cardiac arrest, and a man is flown in after being gored by a tusked boar.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on the night shift as experienced by A&E workers, with exclusive access to Hull Royal Infirmary. The night-time challenges facing NHS staff are captured in their raw form.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Leo faces a life-threatening crisis: his liver is failing and he needs an urgent transplant. Daniel needs surgery for a tumour that could make its way to his lungs.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.desc: In 2021, Taio Cruz was accused of breaking into a house and assaulting the occupants. Having taken a plea deal, the 24-year-old could be sent to jail. Will she show remorse and take responsibility for her actions or is she playing the system?

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.desc: Ross takes up rugby to impress Emily, and Chandler sets up a ruse to avoid his former girlfriend Janice.

programme.desc: Rachel throws a surprise party for Emily so she can invite Joshua; Phoebe develops a craving for meat.

programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: Bath Tubbies

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: The hare nibbles on a pumpkin in the garden! Follow the seed that flies through the air and lands on the ground! In the winter, the rain waters the seed and roots, and a stem grow! In the spring, watch a bee fly between the flowers and in the summer.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present performances by Jaki Graham, The B-52's, Billy Ocean, Patti LaBelle & Michael McDonald, Simply Red, Spitting Image and The Matchroom Mob with Chas & Dave. Also clips from Pete Wylie, AC/DC and Robert Palmer.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 May 1991 and featuring T'Pau, Simple Minds and Deacon Blue.

programme.title: ... Sings Dylan
programme.desc: A look through the archives at some of the many artists who have come into BBC studios to sing their versions of Bob Dylan songs. Featured performers include Peter, Paul and Mary, Lulu, the Byrds, Joan Baez, Eric Clapton, Madeleine Peyroux, Bryan Ferry, UB40, Julie Felix, Manfred Mann, the Brian Auger Trinity and Pops Staples.

programme.title: ...Sings Dylan II
programme.desc: A feast of cover versions of Bob Dylan songs from the BBC archives, with classic tracks old and new and some surprises too from the 1960s to the present. From the essential folk queen Joan Baez to punk princess Siouxsie and the Banshees, from the Hollies to Adele, taking in the likes of Julie Felix, Richie Havens, Bryan Ferry and KT Tunstall along the way, the programme reflects Dylan's long career of writing extraordinary songs.

programme.title: Bob Dylan: Shadow Kingdom
programme.desc: A showcase of Bob Dylan with his band in an intimate setting as he performs songs from his extensive body of work, including `Forever Young', `I'll Be Your Baby Tonight', `It's All Over Now Baby Blue' and many more.

programme.title: Tangled up with Dylan: The Ballad of AJ Weberman
programme.desc: The life, times and crimes of Bob Dylan obsessive, AJ Weberman - an irreverent and witty exploration into one man's obsessions, a bohemian life lived on the New York fringes and a twisted take on the American Dream. Both hilarious and disturbing, the film is an observation on our unbalanced desires to know more about celebrities and how far we are willing to go to get that information.

Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.desc: Celebrating a fantastic week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026, Nicki Chapman and Angellica Bell are joined by Dame Mary Berry to reveal which garden has won the BBC RHS People's Choice Award. Florist to the stars Hamish Powell shows you how to create some fabulous floral displays, and we answer more of your questions in the Chelsea Garden Clinic.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having met online and fallen in love just over a year ago, today's couple are calling on the help of Nicki Chapman to find them their very first home together in the East Sussex countryside. To better understand what living in this county is like, Nicki meets up with one of its residents, none other than fellow Escape to the Country presenter Alistair Appleton. Alistair welcomes Nicki to his beautiful, off-grid, 200-year-old former coastguard's cottage, which he calls home.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop

Can the model capture the spirit of a community that endured so much?

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes Anna to the foodie capital of Ireland. Katherine's memories of her grandmother's over-boiled and under-seasoned meals have left her with a bad impression of Irish food, but Anna is on a mission to change that. First stop is the world-renowned Ballymaloe Cookery School, somewhere that inspired Anna's own culinary journey, followed by a visit to one of the oldest food markets in Europe, the English Market. Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.title: Two Weeks in August

Wesley tries to fit in with the gang by learning what he sees as the Runcornian way of life: eating pasties, shimmying like Tim and borrowing each other's clothes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she's in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway journey through the islands of Sardinia and Corsica sees him leave Sardinia and arrive into Corsica, the most mountainous island in the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a fragile silk robe and a wartime wall painting to a writer's handmade timepiece, the hidden treasures in these two houses reveal the stories of two of Britain's most celebrated authors - and the expert teams racing to preserve them.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge in the tenth heat of the series. The specialist subjects are the life of Anne Lister, Dear Evan Hansen, Elizabeth I and the feature films of David Lynch.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Daisy and Louis struggle to get on the same financial page while learning that making a go of the vineyard is going to take hard work and sacrifice.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox considers what it is about our world that makes it a home for life and asks what ingredients were necessary to transform this once barren planet into the Earth we know today? He reveals that it was a rare chain of events combined with the power of life itself that have made Earth unique amongst the cosmos.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. A Welsh dresser with no drawer handles, a travelling chamber stick used in the Crimean War and a sledge flag from Shackleton's ill-fated Antarctic trip of 1916 cause great excitement for the experts in Tenby.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes, and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round and the scores are tallied.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael boards one of Europe's most dramatic railways, which winds through Corsica's breathtaking mountainous landscapes.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol Klein and Diarmuid Gavin, along with guest judge and gardening fan Fred Sirieix, continue the search to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden. First up is Anne's Kilcootry Barn Gardens in Fintona.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham show how to help the pollinators in your garden and at Beechgrove as they focus on planting that will attract bees.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The annual Royal Horticultural Society Chelsea Flower Show is considered the most famous flower show in the United Kingdom, attracting visitors from all over the world.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.sub-title: The Duke, His Wife and Their Lover
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain's great houses - and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Guz Khan introduces the fabulous Kiri Pritchard-McLean and the amazing Rhys James.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: And Now for Something Completely Different
programme.desc: Silly walks and dead parrots are among the items in this compilation of skits from `Monty Python's Flying Circus'. Watch the team unleash the funniest moments from their early days.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Current affairs programme, featuring interviews and investigative reports on all aspects of life in Britain.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's time for the week's six best cooks to dig deep and deliver in the last quarter-final of the competition.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers head to Somerset to transform sailors' mess decks at Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. They must turn them into vibrant morale-boosting spaces where the young recruits can cook, eat and socialise. Olympic champion and ex-servicewoman Dame Kelly Holmes joins Michelle to judge the rooms.

programme.desc: It's been ages since their last quest, and the heroes are losing their edge, so Friendlyface sends them on a fake quest to get rid of a monster.

programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have set up a long line of dominos in the cabin that ends with several giant ice blocks and a rocket outside. Weary of not finding the peace and calm he loves so dearly, Grizzy sabotages the game. The rocket hits an ice block and blows up the cabin. Grizzy gets his hands on a machine that can sculpt ice. The bear dreams of an igloo, but the Lemmings have other projects.

programme.desc: Karen gets a computer virus, and it is up to SpongeBob to try and get it out of her.

programme.desc: When Duck and Frog get a job in a restaurant, Frog accidentally makes the soup too spicy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: Bitesize: 7-9 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about the orchestra, different timbres, and body percussion.

programme.title: One Zoo Three

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special battle-themed show as Rattus and his siblings are having a huge pillow fight.

programme.title: Go Get Arty

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky is having really weird dreams, which lead to him to make surreal art, including giant hands and a burger hat inspired by Rene Magritte. After scoffing some cheesy snacks, Ricky gets his pyjamas on to create some illuminating dream art.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.sub-title: We Could Actually Stop Climate Change?
programme.desc: We're destroying our planet - but we show that there are things you can do to help save it.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Megan is a wildlife expert who campaigns for animal rights and has a passion for shark conservation. Her mission is to look into the threats facing sharks in British waters and around the globe, from the deadly fin trade to industrial fishing.

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall comes face to face with the tiger shark. Diving with them in the warm waters of the Bahamas, Steve soon discovers they'll use their mouths to investigate potential food.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: How does your body know when you need a wee? And an illusion turns Dr Xand's face green.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Laleeyah lives on the Crow Creek Native American reservation in South Dakota, USA. There aren't many opportunities for people on the reservation, and life can be tough, but Laleeyah loves to learn about the traditions of her ancestors and takes pride in keeping them alive. Once a year there is the Pow Wow: a big, colourful, celebration where the whole Crow Creek tribe comes together to compete and celebrate with traditional dancing, music and dress.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: It's fireworks night, there's a big display at school and everyone is there. The Encore dancers set up a stall to raise money for costumes and have enough sweets and treats for everyone - but they need to sell them all. After months of rehearsals, it's finally opening night for Anything Goes, but will the cast hold their nerve and put on an outstanding performance?

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Goalie Reks steps up to play for the Spurs cerebral palsy men's team.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: While tracking in the Congolese jungle, the Octonauts come upon a mysterious creature.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Nugget takes on a scary new ice slide but loses her nerve, Pancake devises a plan.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have to protect a celebrity on their train from selfie-hungry hordes.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes quest to save the forest from a magical mess.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife expert and nature-lover Steve Backshall sets himself the challenge of meeting 60 of the deadliest and most dangerous animals on the planet, including mammals, reptiles and insects.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Brosmas is here! Callum's Christmas challenges await Adam and Joe in a festive showdown! Gingerbread houses, gift wrapping, tree decorating - Christmas needs a little competition!

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe go out of this world as Callum transforms them into UFO taxi drivers.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.sub-title: Bitzer Puts His Foot in It
programme.desc: The farmer is laying concrete and Bitzer is left to guard it while it dries. However, the wet cement proves too much of a temptation for Shaun and the flock.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis is mega-excited to trial a pair of hi-tech Smarty Pants, underpants with all the capabilities of a smartphone. However, whenever he and the gang test the pants, Walter is always one step ahead of them.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes quest to keep a two-headed dragon away.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The siblings unite to prepare an awesome Christmas surprise for their parents but their efforts are thwarted every step of the way.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings organise a drone race and interrupt Grizzy's planned nap, prompting him to interrupt the competition. Consequently, the bear is thrown out of the cabin.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings disguise themselves as traditional Chinese dancers in order to trick Grizzy and steal the TV.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck soon learns that he is a terrible babysitter, but it seems kids love Frog!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck's has to sneak into a greenhouse to get his plant back after it is mistakenly sold.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton hasn't tried to steal the secret formula in a while...and that's making Mr. Krabs nervous!

programme.title: Submarine Jim
programme.desc: Jim implements various water-saving measures in the submarine.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.sub-title: The Night Mayor; The Big Stink
programme.desc: Omni and Osric are desperate to get off the night shift and go to a party, but they must first stop a mysterious villain; Orwell and Okra must prevent a flying machine from stinking up Odd Squad's favourite places.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Matilda the Musical, and The Mandalorian and Grogu
programme.desc: The RSC production of Matilda the Musical performs When I Grow Up.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: An interactive historical sketch show based on the successful books by Terry Deary. Showcasing interesting facts from the Roman Empire to Medieval times, through to the Victorian era.

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: Southern Africa is a riot of life and colour because of two great ocean currents that sweep around the continent's Cape. To the east, the warm Agulhas current generates clouds that roll inland to the wettest place in southern Africa. To the west is the cold Benguela current, home to more great white sharks than anywhere else. Moisture laden fog rolls inland, supporting an incredible desert garden.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Access All Areas:Behind the Scenes at Radio One Big Weekend
programme.desc: Joel meets some of the incredible acts performing at Radio 1's Big Weekend.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Grapefruit dedicating the song All My Friends to the close pals who go with her everywhere.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Flop are finally ready to leave the house for the picnic they have planned just as it starts to rain. Bing is very disappointed at this turn of events, but as they unpack the wheelie bag, he realises that they can have their picnic indoors instead.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's raining in Teletubbyland so the Teletubbies spend the day eating toast and splashing in puddles, just like the children in Tummy Tales.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is trying to do some gardening, but Tee wants to hunt for treasure!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Chilli must save the amazing Jalapeno when Evil Pea ruins his tart-jumping record attempt.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: While looking for things to sell in their shop, Bing and Sula find their old friend Mr Rainybow, but his head is completely gone. So they decide to sell him, and after a nice lady buys his stacky bit for her toilet rolls, they look for some more things to sell. But then they find Mr Rainybow's head and are sad that they can't get the rest of him back. However, Charlie loves to knock down Mr Rainybow's rings.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Jackson's big, blue chicken-thing, Ludwig, is going to lay an egg. And it's up to Mojo and Bo to carry the big bird across dangerous roads to Preston's egg-hatching shed!

programme.title: Hey Duggee's Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Billy and the Sillies introduce a visit to chicken A&E, the Squirrels build a bug house, Norrie has her chance to shine and the Penguins give facts about what's hot and what's not.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Peggee and the Hummingbirds journey up the river to deliver a parcel, they have fun spotting and avoiding the wildlife that they meet along the way. All while Otti tries to avoid playing with the boat whistle!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: At the end of a long day, Bluey and her family are out of sync and out of sorts, so Mum gets everyone to play musical statues.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Follows the adventures of a Blue Heeler puppy, Bluey, who lives with her mum, dad, and sister. Her energy and lovable spirit gets her into all kinds of funny and unpredictable situations.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The grimbots attach a bit of the Taj Mahal to GrimHQ. Can the Go Jetters fix the glitch?

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Great Breakout
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor captures the whole squirrel tribe in his garden, Peter must stage a mass breakout to save his friends.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts help a group of algae-eating fish stand up to some aggressive damselfish, who are harming their own reef by over-farming it with algae.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The terribly naughty Terrible Twos are making custard pies and Octoblock is all tied up. Can his friends save the day? Discover ways to make eight with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: OI, OR and UR are in a coin-rolling, cork-popping, wave-surfing mad dash to prove who is the fastest! This episode introduces the'-oi-', '-or-' and '-ur-' digraphs and the words join, coin, cork, surf, turf, cord, corn and end.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Today is Popcast sports day. Nick is entering the potato-and-spoon race, but he is having one of those days when everything is going wrong. The Popcasters tell Nick to try his best, but is this enough for him to win a medal?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Incy Wincy Spider
programme.desc: Hedgley sings a song about Incy Wincy Spider.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: It's laundry day in Playtime Towers, and Bella and Lift have washed everyone's socks. All they need to do now is find the matching pairs!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has a great time choosing the perfect present for Sula. He's thrilled until it's time to hand it over - when he decides he wants to keep it for himself.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `fish' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different types of fish: a small fish, a big fish, a scaly fish and a gold fish. We meet some stripy fish, a spiky fish, and even see some jumping fish.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: While on a boat trip with Gus and his grandad, Lu loses Tara due to her constant rushing.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri is having an ordinary kind of day and doesn't feel like doing much. But Pania's extraordinary passing of wind makes the day amazing.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: The Froglets enjoy a fin-tastic fishing adventure and end up with an unexpected catch.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos learn all about getting wet when they have an encounter with a garden hose. The Box brings them a rainy cloud, which makes the Boat wet too!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: Keep it Colourful
programme.desc: It's a colourful day out for Maddie and friends, while Mr Tumble decorates a cake.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are trying very hard not to make any noise. Then they make as much noise as possible during the Tubby Phone Dance.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies George, Inam, Isaac, Hunter, Luther, Billie and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyone's bags in this episode are paintbrushes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding an appropriate paintbrush inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song 'Finger Family' features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children make woolly lambs and sign and sing 'Mary Had a Little Lamb'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Aaron is joined by Jackson and Mia as they discover how to feed the garden, and the special role wiggly worms have in making sure the plants stay happy and healthy.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is trying to win a lasso contest, but first, there's some making to be done with Murphi. A toothpaste box and a toothpaste tube are transformed into a toothpaste tube snake, the Mini Monsters create something beastly out of burger boxes, and Rebecca's cousin calls on the Magic Monster Mirror. Finally, a large cardboard tube provides inspiration for a terrific tube vulture.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is overwhelmed by all the options at the markets with her five dollars from the tooth fairy. But when she and Indy search for the perfect purchase, spending it proves hard.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels notice that Happy looks and seems different - he is frowning, his smile is upside down, and he's wearing new trainers! When they check in on him, Happy is frustrated, but he can't seem to identify where his frustration is coming from. They turn to Duggee for help, who uses his feeling cards to explain an array of feelings to Happy. All the Squirrels help by relaying their past experiences and the feelings that they evoked.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Making Music
programme.desc: We are making music! Ha! Ha!, we are making music!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ted and his family go on a walk through the woods and everyone joins in a big bug hunt. When they come across a bridge over a stream, they decide to have an epic game of pooh sticks.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Banoffi pie was thought to have been invented in Sussex, and today Sonny-Mac is showing us how to make it. Sonny-Mac puts bananas and cream on top of a tasty base of crushed biscuits. He also adds his special ingredient, dates, to make it lovely and sweet. Not all his friends like the taste of dates at first, so let's hope they enjoy them when the pie is ready. Sonny-Mac serves the pie to his friends at a special tea party, where everyone dresses very smartly.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a singing group and the children help us find out how we use our mouths, vocal chords and lungs to make different sounds when we sing and speak. Maddie uses a special machine to measure the different high and low sounds that we make. Next, she visits a trombone factory to see how copper and zinc metals are made into brass trombones.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Charli and her disability assistance dog Tinks visit a Hobby Fair at Woofton Town Hall, where Charli is selling all the clever things she makes.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Tamarin and Crowned Pigeon
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet some miniature monkeys, whose haircut gives them their name cotton-top tamarins. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie head out to see the crowned pigeon. He's a little shy, but eventually he comes out to meet them.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gramps, Evie and Waffle arrive home with Ruby and Jelly for a playdate just as a thousand, accidentally ordered, toilet rolls are delivered to Mrs Hobbs' house next door.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: It's a hot day on Puffin Rock and everyone is struggling to stay cool. When Baba finds some of Flynne's fur, Oona becomes convinced that Flynne must need help.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated preschool series that features four adorable potatoes who sing and dance as they explore.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee discovers a little mouse who is lost in the woods. A musical song map may help Frida find her way back home and never get lost again.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie's fun and games are temporarily stopped when Triggs' incredibly waggy tail starts accidentally knocking things over - it's almost like his waggy tail has a life of its own!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are crafting in the Clubhouse when Betty saves Tag from throwing water across the table instead of glitter. Tag, although full of enthusiasm and energy, has not always been the best listener. He was great at discovering new things, and everyone wanted to join in with him - but getting easily distracted would land him in an array of trouble.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks show us what they are made of in a song and dance all about the parts that make a whole. Find out how to split numbers into smaller numbers with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. From A to Z, every `Alphablock' has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. When O and F turn the lights off, how will they get them back on?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble and Fisherman Tumble plan a picnic by the lake, but the food keeps disappearing! Meanwhile, Justin takes his friends to a pond, where they have fun with nets.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po collects lots of balloons and takes off into the air! Floating high above Teletubbyland, Po waves at Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa below. When the balloons pop, Po lands.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how beetles play an important role in the environment. They find out that beetles do so many amazing jobs, like pollinating plants and eating and recycling the nutrients in things like dead wood. They also find out that they are an important part of the food chain and that some beetles are predators who help keep pests under control. On the way, they spin like whirligig beetles and search for dung beetles in cow pats!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move in a really cool way. Grace has a drive in a lowrider and makes it bounce, shows off a car that can drive over other cars in a traffic jam, and does some donuts in a drift car.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: Dodge's new water bottle cover starts a trend within the class.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster worries about showing people his favourite conch shell when everyone else has unicorn-themed items for their show and tell presentations.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Paani seeks refuge in a grouse's burrow during a snowstorm but then gets trapped.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Mum's Precious Things
programme.desc: When Peter accidentally discards his mother's most precious possessions he must retrieve them from Tommy Brock!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are having fun playing games but are surprised to find out that Betty didn't always enjoy playing. Since her first day at Squirrel Club, she has always impressed everyone with her confidence and independence, but because she had grown up playing alone, she didn't know how to join in with the others. Luckily, Duggee had a great idea to bring all the Squirrels together. As a group, they performed the Squirrel Club song about their friends and what they're good at.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Can Sylvie the butterfly join in with the Puddle Parade without getting her wings wet?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea plunges Broccoli and Carrot into the doughnut toppings simultaneously, and Supertato almost quits being a superhero when he can't save his two friends at the same time.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A shark on the loose in the streets of Swoppiton? Mojo and Bo are on a fishing trip to find out more! But the hungry shark may not be quite as hungry-sharky as they think.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: At the end of a long day, Bluey and her family are out of sync and out of sorts, so Mum gets everyone to play musical statues.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Follows the adventures of a Blue Heeler puppy, Bluey, who lives with her mum, dad, and sister. Her energy and lovable spirit gets her into all kinds of funny and unpredictable situations.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Gadabout gives himself dance fever to prove he is as much fun as Zog and Pearl. When he can't stop boogieing, they must cure him before sunset so he is not stuck dancing forever!

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum jumps to conclusions when he hears about an upcoming volcanic eruption. But little does he know, it's Ava's science project that's set to fizzle in a safe experiment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Waffle's birthday and Mr Willow throws a huge party for him. As the Brooklyn-Bells prepare a surprise for Waffle, Connie and Ruby's dog, Jelly, leads the Wonder Dog astray.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: It's raining, so Rafi and Ruby are playing indoor football. But when they accidentally break a plate, Rafi wishes for it to be fixed but warns them not to play again.

programme.sub-title: It's Time to Press a Flower
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran press a flower so that it will last for a long time. JoJo learns that flowers wilt over time, but flower pressing is a special way to make them last longer. JoJo and Gran Gran look through Gran Gran's wedding album and enjoy old photographs, special things and happy memories. Gran Gran realises they've found the perfect place to keep their special pressed flower.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Rainbow's colourful glow inspires an art project.

programme.desc: A strange, flying metal caravan lands on the Clangers' planet. It turns out to be a travelling shop, and its owner is a robot salesman. He has things he thinks the Clangers will like, and he's right - they do. However, they don't realise at first that the salesman wants to be paid. And when they do, they of course don't have any money. The dilemma is resolved when the robot spots the one thing he would really like in return.

programme.desc: When Choudhary Amar Singh is killed because of the money he owes a cruel landowner, his son decides to avenge him.

programme.title: Pyar Ki Ladai
programme.desc: A jobless man spends his time doing unfruitful things, but his life changes when he falls in love with the sister of a sarpanch. However, to marry her, he must win a kabaddi match.

programme.title: Kya Yehi Pyaar Hai
programme.desc: A woman falls in love with her fiancé's brother after inviting him to return home.

programme.title: Dil Ka Rishta
programme.desc: Jai is hurt when Tia introduces her fiancé, Raj. A drunk Jai drives a vehicle straight into Tia and Raj, killing Raj. Tia recovers but doesn't remember.

programme.title: Phir Hera Pheri
programme.desc: Greed for more and easy money takes the rags-to-riches men Baburao, Shyam and Raju back to bankruptcy.

programme.title: Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani
programme.desc: A young medical student falls in love with a man who plans to travel the world and never settle down or get married.

programme.title: Main Hoon Na
programme.desc: Major Ram is assigned with two missions, one is to protect a fragile peace initiative with Pakistan called Project Milap, and the other is to protect the daughter of General Bakshi, the commander in charge of Project Milap. The project takes him to St. Paul's College in Darjeeling, where he must go undercover as a student. As a mature student, Major Ram becomes a misfit with the hip college students.

programme.title: Saajan
programme.desc: A poet discovers that he and his best friend are in love with the same woman.

programme.title: Kochadaiyaan
programme.desc: Rana, a ruthless warrior, returns to his homeland to avenge the death of his father, who was unjustly put to death by the reigning king of Kotaipattinam.

programme.title: 777 Charlie
programme.desc: A naughty and energetic pup named Charlie gives a lonely and reclusive man a new perspective toward his life.

programme.title: Motichoor Chaknachoor
programme.desc: A woman whose single purpose in life is to marry a foreign-settled groom decides to marry a 36-year-old Dubai-returned professional who is desperate for a bride.

programme.title: Ishqiya
programme.desc: While on the run, Kalujaan and Babban fall for a kidnapper's seductive widow. The three plan to commit a crime unaware of the fate that awaits them.

programme.title: Housefull
programme.desc: A man, known for being unlucky, feels like there is no other way but to end his life. Then he meets a beautiful woman, but as it turns out, this woman may bring him the worst luck he has ever experienced.

programme.title: Jab We Met
programme.desc: Aditya is a business scion whose life is going all wrong. Geet is a bubbly girl who couldn't care less about life. Fate brings them together on a life-changing journey.

programme.title: Ok Jaanu
programme.desc: Two ambitious people fall in love after meeting at a wedding. They decide on a no-strings-attached live-in relationship, but doubt their decision when their careers pull them apart.

programme.title: Khakee
programme.desc: DCP Anant Shrivastav is assigned on a mission of transferring an alleged terrorist from Chandangadh to Mumbai. He assembles a diverse group of officers to aid him with a task. The mission is both dangerous and life-changing.

programme.title: Joroo Ka Ghulam
programme.desc: Kalpana and Rajesh have a chance encounter, which eventually leads to love. Her father is opposed to their marriage, but the pair defy him.

programme.desc: Debra takes it upon herself to prepare the Thanksgiving dinner but, not wishing to have her turkey compared to Marie's, decides to cook fish. Threatened by Debra's unorthodox plan, Marie resorts to extreme culinary measures.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds out that her picture of her father, whom she has never known, is just a generic photograph that comes with the picture frame. Elsewhere, after Ross makes Monica's heater malfunction, her Christmas party guests swelter in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets a great acting opportunity on `Days of our Lives', Rachel dates a guy called Russ who is amazingly similar to Ross, and Monica renews her relationship with `Fun' Bobby, but finds out Bobby is only fun when he is drinking.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding
programme.desc: Rachel's free-spirited mother visits and reveals that she wants to end her marriage and experiment sexually. Meanwhile, Carol and Susan prepare for their wedding, with some help from an uncomfortable Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Joey starts dating a fan who is gorgeous, but soon discovers that she's also quite mad. Meanwhile, Ross is reminded how much he misses Marcel his old pet monkey, and travels to San Diego Zoo to find out what has happened to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross finally spends some time with Marcel after many false starts, and Chandler is delighted to get a date with the sexy Susie, who he hasn't seen since fourth grade. However, she gets her revenge for youthful humiliation by leaving him in the loo, naked but for a pair of her knickers.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Prom Video
programme.desc: When Monica's parents clear out her old room to install a gym, they unearth an old video of her and Rachel's first prom night. It's obvious from this that Ross is in love with Rachel and, having told him it will never happen, Rachel changes her mind. Elsewhere, Joey gives Chandler a hideous bracelet as a token of his friendship and is mortally offended to hear Chandler saying how terrible it is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel ... You Know
programme.desc: Among the guests at Monica's party is a handsome and sophisticated doctor, but the only problem is that he's old enough to be her father. Meanwhile, Joey buys a big screen TV and two reclining leather armchairs with the money from his `Days of Our Lives' contract.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Everyone at Monica's dad's birthday party finds it amusing that their middle-aged friend, eye-doctor Richard Burke, is dating a woman in her 20s - until Monica's parents learn that it is their daughter. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey row over a dirty spoon. Is the apartment still big enough for both of them?

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Moves In
programme.desc: With Joey in his new apartment, Chandler gets a troublesome new flatmate, Eddie. Meanwhile, Phoebe makes a video for `Smelly Cat', and Ross and Monica find it nearly impossible to live together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Dr Ramoray Dies
programme.desc: Joey's remarks to a fan magazine could cause him to lose his job playing Dr Ramoray on a daytime television drama, and Chandler's new flatmate Eddie becomes too annoying to cope with.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Won't Go
programme.desc: Chandler wants his annoying new roommate to move out, Joey is forced to face some exorbitant bills, and the girls learn from a book about personal empowerment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Old Yeller Dies
programme.desc: Monica's new boyfriend impresses Chandler and Joey, Phoebe finds out what she missed as a child, and Rachel resents the fact that Ross is planning the rest of their lives together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is broke and decides to gamble on the stock market, and Chandler and Ross run into a couple of bullies in Central Park. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey help Phoebe locate her father, but a vicious dog prevents Phoebe from finally meeting him. After accidentally injuring the dog with her car, Phoebe gets to know her father's second wife whom she never knew about, and another long-lost family member.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides to hold a surprise birthday for Rachel, but everything goes wrong when Rachel's estranged parents turn up unexpectedly. The party then splits into two factions: a dreary gathering in Monica's apartment, and a college-style drunken party at Chandler and Joey's flat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Chicken Pox
programme.desc: Phoebe's submariner boyfriend visits New York on leave from the Navy. Unfortunately, Phoebe catches chicken pox at baby Ben's first birthday party. Meanwhile, when Monica realises that Richard has no real neuroses, she worries that she is too strange for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindy's Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiancé Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Story Continues
programme.desc: Kevin returns to the SECC to perform a record-breaking run of ten sold out nights to over 75,000 ecstatic fans. Recorded on a Saturday night in Glasgow, Kevin is in his element, as this home town gig shows off his no-nonsense approach to comedy.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Absurd comedy about a likeable buffoon who turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment. Mr Bean rushes to his exam only to find out he's studied the wrong subject. He then goes to the beach but struggles to change into his swimming trunks discretely. Later on, he has difficulty attending a church service without drawing attention to himself.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean wants to give a busker some money until he realises he has no change, while afterwards he goes shopping but gets carried away. Later, he treats himself to a birthday dinner but is disappointed with his dish. Finally, Mr Bean attends a royal film premiere in order to meet the Queen, with disastrous results.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes to a swimming pool but finds himself terrified of the high diving board; afterwards, he must try several imaginative ways to get out of the car park when he doesn't have enough money to pay. He then demonstrates the proper way to make a sandwich whilst out at the park. Finally, when Mr Bean and his girlfriend go on a date to watch a horror movie he teases her for being scared, until the film actually starts.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean buys a portable television but can't get it to work properly. Then, when his camera is stolen, he must identify the suspect in a police line up. Afterwards, he attends a magic show and disco with his girlfriend but ends up embarrassing her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Underdog Gets Away
programme.desc: Monica's first Thanksgiving dinner for the gang goes horribly wrong when they all get locked on the roof after watching a giant runaway balloon from the New York Thanksgiving parade. Elsewhere, Joey wins his first modelling gig as the poster boy for a venereal disease campaign, and Ross competes with his ex-wife to communicate with their unborn baby.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: As New Year approaches, the gang make a pact to celebrate at Monica and Rachel's flat without bringing any dates. However, as the New Year gets closer, all of them find themselves breaking that promise except for Ross, who brings along his new pet monkey Marcel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler's flamboyant mother visits New York, while Phoebe and Monica flirt with a handsome stranger while crossing a road and end up causing a traffic accident.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Dozen Lasagnes
programme.desc: Rachel's Italian boyfriend tries to seduce Phoebe, Rachel accidentally reveals a secret to Ross, and Monica's aunt refuses to take the lasagnes she ordered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler sees Rachel nude, and Joey learns that his father is having an affair.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Candy Hearts
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day and, out on his first date in nine years, Ross meets up with his ex-wife. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe and Rachel rebel against the spirit of the day by burning mementos of past relationships.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Stoned Guy
programme.desc: The owner of a fancy restaurant where Monica applies for the chef's position is a little high on illicit substances when he tries the gourmet feast she prepares for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe doesn't get on with her identical twin Ursula. Unfortunately, no one can tell them apart, which leads to a few cases of mistaken identity, not least for Joey who is quite taken with Ursula.

programme.title: Dr. Dolittle 2
programme.desc: A group of beavers, headed by a Godfather-style leader, ask Dolittle to save their habitat from loggers. The only hope is to get the forest preserved because it's the home of a protected bear, but there's a problem: the bear's the only bear in the forest, so she can't reproduce. Undaunted, Dolittle persuades a circus bear to help out, but he has to teach him not just the ways of the wild, but the wiles of lady bears too.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh are back on the road for another epic road trip, and this time they're in for a real culture shock as they head further afield to Latin America. The boys are in Mexico, where they take on Lucha Libre wresting.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes continue their Latin American road trip. They're still in Mexico and on the side of a mountain - we rejoin them to see if Bradley can complete the final part of a challenge which requires him to swing across the top of the mountain with nothing but a terrifying drop below him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After weeks of procrastinating, Rachel finally takes down the Christmas lights, but sprains her ankle.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Poker

programme.sub-title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away
programme.desc: Rachel accidentally lets Ross' pet monkey escape, and the friends form a search party. She is then horrified to learn that her former best friend is engaged to marry her ex-fiancé.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiancé Barry, even though he's engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Join a candid chat with the Golds as they discuss Les's discovery of a jewel-filled bag in an employee's desk.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Look back with the Golds as they recall their moves, biggest blunders and irresistible deals.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Discover how the Golds truly react when a customer attempts to swindle the shop.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Discover Ashley and Seth's thoughts on Les's return to form after his operation.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: When jewellery is found strewn about and the security video system conveniently fails, Les suspects foul play.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes with the Golds as they recall Tressa, a former employee and Ashley's nemesis, and her plot to swindle American Jewellery out of a valuable Rolex.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Get up close and personal with the Golds as the family visits Les's grandfather's closed-down pawnshop in downtown Detroit, and Les recounts the story of a frightening stick-up.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Series following the colourful life of a large pawn shop. From alligators to false limbs, its staff has no idea what could come through the door next, and profits come with a huge risk.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Stockport with dealers Simon Schneider, Karen Dalmeny, David Hakeney and Brenda Haller, Brenda is desperate to pin down a deal for an antique gold brooch while Karen plays host to a teddy bears' picnic and Simon cannot believe his luck when an all too desirable Royal Lancastrian vase lands on his table.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers visit Liverpool Cathedral, where some girls give Henry Nicholls a run for his money, some photos land on Cheryl Hakeney's table and will Tony Geering let a 1930s cocktail shaker go to auction?

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Matlock in Derbyshire with his team of Tim Hogarth, Brenda Haller, James Layte and Tony Geering. A set of Chinese paintings cause a stir in the room.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers Corrie Jeffery, David Hakeney, Helen Gardiner and David Ford are in Ludlow in Shropshire, where items of interest include an Omega watch, a walking stick with an ivory handle carved into a dog's face, and a strange object called a potato flask.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his team including Corrie Jeffrey, James Layte, David Hakeney and Debbie Serpell are in Oldham today where a fascinating medal turns up on James's table.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his dealers are in Winsford where Helen Gardiner discovers that clinching a deal for two rare German toys is far from child's play and Henry Nicholls gets a blast from the past from a retro phone.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and the team are in Blackburn where they come face to face with a tiger, other items of interest include a Doulton facemask which Corrie Jeffrey worries she has been charmed by, and Mark Stevens meets his match when he haggles with a father and son over a gentleman's wristwatch.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his dealers Michael Hogben, Mark Stevens, Stewart Hofgartner and Corrie Jeffrey are in Blackburn. Items of interest include a Victorian wax seal, a Clarice Cliff jug and an obscure telephone repair kit.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Burton-on-Trent is the location today as a naive marine painting comes through the door - but is the valuation as naive? and there are some saucy postcards that make Jo Brayshaw chuckle.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Skipton is the location today as Cheryl Hakeney gets herself in a spin over an Art Deco bronze figure, other items brought along include a Royal Doulton Toby jug, and a cavalier sword.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Barrow-in-Furness, where Tim Hogarth meets a young wannabe dealer who certainly knows how to play the game and Stewart Hofgartner comes face-to-face with a seller who brings in an absolute bargain.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are in Carlisle, where rock `n' roll autographs attract strong bidding at auction, and Helen Gardiner's Scottish roots push her to offering big money for a ceremonial dirk.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are in Carlisle, where rock `n' roll autographs attract strong bidding at auction, and Helen Gardiner's Scottish roots push her to offering big money for a ceremonial dirk.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind The Deal
programme.desc: Some of the greatest Hardcore Pawn episodes through the eyes of the Golds.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Miami Heat player Dwyane Wade surprises a Florida resident playing for $1 million; a door-to-door salesman tries his luck at becoming a millionaire.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: A St. Louis preschool teacher gets a surprise appearance as she plays for the top prize of $1 million.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.
