programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: Shawn strikes a deal with Dave Young that comes with a ticking clock. Zeke's new dredge Black Swan debuts on the Bering Sea. Kris' backup plan wildly backfires. Vernon and Emily seek out their own claims.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger search in an unlikely location for the ideal spot to sea-age their Tennessee Whiskey. A wrong turn earns Josh his next broken bone. When Jerry gets rushed to the ER, his partner is forced to figure out a complex recipe without him.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Horseshoes, dishwashers, graphite fly rods and frozen pizzas: more everyday items are put under the microscope to find out how they are made.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Pistons, paint rollers, parachutes and chimneys: more everyday items are put under the microscope to find out how they are made.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete works his magic in Georgia to build a treehouse the breaks all that country rules, and then visits racing legend Dale Earnhardt Jr.'s own treehouse.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete and the team give a thrill-seeking couple their dream treehouse - straddling three cedars with a wraparound deck providing 360 degree of stunning views.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Parker's Trail
programme.desc: Parker strikes gold in Bolivia at a location that could be the next Klondike; with time running out on his prospect, Parker faces a big decision.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Parker's Trail
programme.desc: Parker Schnabel travels the gold-filled Madeira River deep into the Brazilian wilderness as he hunts for a fleet of dredges bringing in billions.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A couple in New Mexico plan to construct a self-sufficient structure, but with blowing dirt and variable temperatures they may have underestimated the desert.

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: An adorable baby hippo arrives and an orphaned koala needs support. Plus, a team travels to Kenya to share their innovative milk formula for elephant calves.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Seaweed is made into sushi wrapping, beer bottles are crafted to last fifteen years and special techniques are employed to keep surfers safe from man-eating sharks.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Explore the methods used to cast the street lamps of Venice, prevent another Titanic like disaster and grow tiny trees for the ancient Chinese art of penjing.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Racing Bikes; Stunt Pilots; Oil Well
programme.desc: Racing bikes, stunt pilots and oil wells.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Bagels, racing tyres and liuli glass.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue: Raney Ranch
programme.desc: In a race against the coming snow, the Raneys push beyond their limits to finish two cabins, harvest the garden and mount a hunting expedition. When a risky project results in a devastating injury, the family is in danger of losing it all.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue: Raney Ranch
programme.desc: As Marty recovers from a bad fall, the Raneys scramble against the oncoming winter to finish cabin walls and build a staircase from an ancient family spruce. Matt leads a last-ditch moose hunt pivotal to keeping them stocked with meat through spring.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy & Juan
programme.desc: Freddy and Juan face off at a hard rock mine spanning three elevations at 4,000 feet in the Cascades; battling impenetrable rock, the guys devise an innovative 180-foot chute featuring a spiral concentrator in their first Washington state rescue.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Phil and Heather head to Belmonte Castle in La Mancha, Spain, said to be haunted by the Spanish Boogeyman. Inside the castle, they discover surprising secrets that reveal the truth behind the terrifying legend.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew tests a disturbing theory linking a secret invisible weapon in Venezuela to unexplained attacks on U.S. officials overseas and Havana Syndrome.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Phil and Jess investigate an abandoned gold mining town haunted by its inhabitants from over a century ago. Traveling deep inside the town's deserted mines, they uncover the town's blood-soaked secrets and a multitude of paranormal activity.

programme.title: Border Control Poland
programme.desc: The security and customs services at the border of Poland are on constant watch to stop illegal items entering the country. It's their job to detect criminals trying to smuggle items.

programme.title: Expedition X

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew tests a disturbing theory linking a secret invisible weapon in Venezuela to unexplained attacks on U.S. officials overseas and Havana Syndrome.

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: The first storm of summer arrives to rock the fleet. New rivals Chris and Joe battle over big gold. Vern's diver faces a deep-water emergency; Kris orders the Mistress to put her crew on notice; and Shawn makes a promising discovery.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger revisit the abandoned theme park of their misspent youth and convert a carnival ride into a whiskey-aging machine.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Do dogs feel guilty when they do a trick? What are the strangest eggs in the world?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations explain the bizarre, the brilliant and the most mind-blowing animal behaviours.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations reveal how a lizard can literally walk on water. And, can jellyfish live forever?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A bird expert reveals how a barn owl catches prey it can't even see. And, what makes octopuses masters of disguise?

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr. Jeff and his team of 30 experts preside over one of the busiest animal clinics in the U.S.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Nichols treats a cat injured in a fight with the dog next door, and a class of fourth-graders count on Dr Jeff to save their pet hedgehog.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: When a dog is rushed into the clinic foaming at the mouth, Dr Jeff must act fast. And when he meets Penelope the Pig, he faces a surgery he's never done before.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Wolfpack arrives on their new property in the most remote frontier of the lower 48. However, the long winter wreaks havoc on the family's attempt to settle in and tame the mountain.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns begin an ambitious barn build, the key to Billy's grand vision. Plus, Noah returns, reuniting the entire wolfpack for the first time since Alaska.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Disaster strikes as floods plague the region, and the Brown's must balance the fate of their new community with the success of their homestead.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Noah invents an ingenious device to rid their homesite of moles. Billy gives daughter Rain major responsibility as barn construction goes full bore at the ranch.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Ami prepares for a critical medical check-up, but Billy has concerns about progress on the homestead while they are away. A rogue bear destroys Bam's new beehives forcing the youngsters to rebuild it.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: In the season premiere, the captains find a rare strain of red King crab in the north amid crushing ice and violent storms. Sig deploys an underwater drone to pinpoint the horde.

programme.title: The Woodland Workshop
programme.desc: Alex builds a wooden soapbox car modelled on a classic 1960s Ferrari. His ambitious design means he needs a little help to get him to the finish line.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search or automotive bargains. They come, they see, they haggle, they repair and finally sell for a tidy profit.

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: A devastating rail disaster outside a small North Dakota town results in cargo erupting in massive explosions; experts use the latest science to investigate and uncover an engineering flaw poised for catastrophic failure.

programme.desc: The Ridler car's engine and transmission are married for the last time and installed. The team moves on to the wiring and other systems.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The boys fix up a BMW 335i estate with the performance and styling that is expected from an M3. They get it in shape to beat a real M3 in a head-to-head drag race.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search or automotive bargains. Today, Henry and Sam discover a collection of motorbikes on Bodmin Moor accumulated over 25 years. They snap up a Suzuki B120 but selling it on to Henry's mate means they hardly make any money on it. Later, in Redruth, the pair come across Harry Potter's Ford Anglia. They bid for it and take it home where a buyer is not far away - their soundman Tommy wants it.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: The boys are back in town and travelling to Redruth, where they're confronted with Roy's sheds and their ramshackle contents. There's everything from a model canary-yellow Lamborghini to Harry Potter's Anglia!

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer enrolls in a `How to Succeed in Being You' course and takes a new attitude to work, where he makes suggestions to improve safety at the power plant. When Burns laughs at Homer's proposals, Homer and Lisa devise a plan to take the plant from him. After being legally outmanoeuvred, Burns signs the plant over to Homer, and Homer becomes the new CEO.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: 'Scuse Me While I Miss the Sky
programme.desc: A British film-maker comes to Bart's class to make a documentary on the typical American school. Moreover, the film-maker leaves Lisa with feelings of inadequacy, causing her to run out and choose a career in astronomy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is delighted with the critical acclaim Marge receives for a novel she writes until he discovers the plot.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: First Jon renovates the family's home for a successful sale, while Drew hunts down the best options for the family's next property and oversees the selling and buying.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby fan Andy and dental nurse Holly bring a Mexican fiesta to Yorkshire. Mitesh and Jess serve an Indian-inspired meal to spice-averse guests. And, Paul and Mandy dish out a few surprises.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Robbie Williams superfan Trish and Dave, quirky Patrick and Lizzie, and seasoned hosts John and Lynne compete in Stoke, in an edition featuring wartime food and a good old knees-up.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Gloucester rugby lad Josh is desperate to please his demanding bride-to-be, Stacie. They met five years ago in a nightclub and Josh popped the question in 2010. Money is tight, so they are given £12,000 pounds for their big day. However, they have agreed that Josh will arrange the wedding on his own and Stacie won't know a single thing until the big day in three weeks' time.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Paintballing Hen Do
programme.desc: It was love at first sight for car sprayer Cameron and teaching assistant Sarah when they met in their local pub. Cameron is one of the last in the family to marry, and struggles to please everybody as he plans the perfect wedding.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: True Value CEO John Hartmann, a former FBI agent, goes under cover and is shocked by the antics of an employee.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Pittsburgh Mayor Bill Peduto tackles undercover roles of municipal employees including a refuse worker, a carpenter and a self-sufficiency coordinator.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is back in Woodland Park, Colorado, for the conclusion of his attempt to help struggling Italian restaurant Mangia Mangia avoid ruin. Will harassed owner Julie ever be reconciled with her out-of-control head chef?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon's in Redondo Beach, California, to visit Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece duo Faye and Brenda.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Fed up with her DIY disaster after nearly a decade, hardworking single mum Catherine is determined to finally flip the problematic property.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In County Antrim, newlyweds Rebecca and Gareth, Nel and Billy, and Mandy and Jim compete in a week featuring a Mexican fiesta, risky puddings and a vegetarian who doesn't like vegetables.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton delivers a timely message of peace. Joanne McNally invents a new catchphrase. Nick Mohammed and Sophie Willan both struggle with a bottle of water. John Robins gets creative with beans. And Alex Horne feels the thrill of victory.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr host a new edition of the panel game, with Sean Lock and Freddie Flintoff who take on special guest captain Bill Bailey with Roisin Conaty.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will's mum is away for a weekend with old schoolfriend Fergus. Will is left home alone and the other boys take full advantage of the situation. They hammer the Ocado account, empty the fridge and rifle through Polly's knicker drawer among other things, but despite this there's a chance even Will might have a good time. That is of course until morning.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will decides to organise one last trip for the gang before they go their separate ways. Our four heroes pack the clapped-out Fiat for a camping trip in the countryside. Will draws on his scout training, the same however, cannot be said for Jay, Neil and Simon who aren't prepared for the great outdoors at all.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch deals with Lee's World Cup wobble. Pash sorts Daniel's not-so-flattering memento. Uzzi covers Harry's catty catchphrase and smokes out Chelsea's slip-up.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Liverpool, Pam and Joey, Mike and Gaynor, and Ste and Danielle compete to host the best dinner party, in a week of confusing food, fishcake misunderstandings and hidden talents.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Northamptonshire, loved-up luvvies Anna and Coylah, sporty Felicity and Lawrence and well-to-do Martin and Dawn compete in a week featuring unthawed prawns, a panto, and limelight theft.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Drag Queens
programme.desc: Dan tries to plan the perfect day for his partner Mark. Despite being engaged, their plans have been on ice due to lack of cash, but now Mark has decided he's going to hand over control of his big day to Dan. Will best man Steve derail the wedding plans?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Newquaye Laura and Mitch are desperate to walk down the aisle, but there's another love in Mitch's life, one that threatens to come between Laura and her dream wedding. Surf addict Mitch must plan a wedding with one eye permanently on the waves.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a Boarding School
programme.desc: Bedfordshire couple Mercy and James have been together for 13 years and got engaged in 2009. Mercy comes from a Nigerian background, so it is a matter of family pride that James gets the traditions just right. How will this most laidback of grooms know what colour tribal outfits to buy and will his lack of planning undo his `I Do'?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Kidderminster country boy Nathan attempts to blow away bride-to-be Nicky with his unique take on a farm wedding. They met seven years ago at a nightclub in Dudley and have been inseparable ever since, but Nathan is not a born romantic. Will he be a fish out of water in the wonderful world of bridal dresses and women's lingerie?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: After just 11 months together, Hywel proposed to Beca at a Blur gig. This trainee social worker wants to rock her world as he tries to bring Glastonbury to Bridgend for their wedding day.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Shane Evans is COO and co-founder of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America. She goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund her own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Bruce `Two Dogs' Bozsum of Mohegan Sun, a Native American gaming and entertainment empire owned and operated by the Mohegan tribe of Connecticut, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon's in Redondo Beach, California, to visit Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece duo Faye and Brenda.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is back in Redondo Beach, California, for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill from imploding, and he still faces more questions than answers.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Easton, Pennsylvania, to visit struggling Italian restaurant Bella Luna. Rosaria bought the establishment to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Ramsay revisits Boston's La Galleria, the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Mass., and the Prohibition Gastropub in Everett, Wash.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels in the USA, aiming to fix hopeless hotels, mediocre motels and bed & breakfasts with bad standards. In the first programme, Gordon visits Coeur d'Alene, Idaho, to try to rescue the failing Roosevelt Inn. Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the Roosevelt's owner, John Hough, is more interested in telling jokes and dressing up as Sherlock Holmes than he is in fixing his struggling B&B.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with `Countdown' regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones. Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed, is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire is dubious of Phil's father when he pays a visit with a dog and RV in tow. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria take Manny to see a horror movie and Cameron fills a temporary void in Dylan's band.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Franklyn
programme.desc: A masked vigilante detective searches for his nemesis on the streets of Meanwhile City, a monolithic fantasy metropolis ruthlessly governed by faith and religious fervour.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Mouse That Roared
programme.desc: Classic farce in which the tiny Grand Duchy of Fenwick declares war on the United States with the aim of glorious defeat and a hefty sum in post-war aid. An advance party travels to New York and kidnaps the inventor of the Q-bomb, unfortunately forcing the Americans into immediate surrender.

programme.title: Written on the Wind
programme.desc: Emotional upheavals abound for a rich Texas oil family when their son and his childhood friend fall for the same woman.

programme.title: Hail the Conquering Hero
programme.desc: Preston Sturges' satire about a sickly soldier discharged from duty and mistaken for a war hero when he returns home.

programme.title: The Story of Robin Hood and His Merrie Men
programme.desc: Robin Hood, the dashing rogue, embarks on a legendary campaign against the evil Prince John and the Sheriff of Nottingham.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Apocalypse Now
programme.desc: The definitive examination of the American experience in the Vietnam War, which centres on captain Benjamin Willard who is sent up river to find and kill insane renegade colonel Walter Kurtz.

programme.title: Underwater
programme.desc: Disaster strikes more than six miles below the ocean surface when water crashes through the walls of a drilling station. Led by their captain, the survivors realise that their only hope is to walk across the sea floor to reach the main part of the facility. But they soon find themselves in a fight for their lives when they come under attack from mysterious and deadly creatures that no one has ever seen.

programme.title: Thief
programme.desc: After a stint in prison, a diamond thief has high hopes of going straight, until he is offered a job he can't refuse.

programme.title: Patrick
programme.desc: A teacher's life gets turned upside down when she inherits her grandmother's cute but mischievous pug.

programme.title: Secret Headquarters
programme.desc: A kid discovers the secret headquarters of a powerful superhero hidden beneath his home and must defend it with his group of friends when villains attack.

programme.title: The Black Swan
programme.desc: With the pardon of notorious pirate Henry Morgan by the English King, Caribbean pirates are offered amnesty if they give up their lawless ways. As governor of Jamaica, Morgan picks Captain Jamie Waring to round up the renegade captain Billy Leech. Beautiful Margaret Denby complicates matters for Waring, as her scorn for him is matched only by his love for her.

programme.title: Romancing the Stone
programme.desc: A dowdy romantic-adventure writer is hurled into a real-life adventure in the Colombian jungle in order to save her sister, who will be killed if a treasure map is not delivered to her captors. She is helped out by a brash mercenary, and together they search for the priceless gem located in the map.

programme.title: Fisherman's Friends
programme.desc: A cynical London music executive hears a singing group of ten Cornish fisherman while on a stag weekend and believes that they can achieve a top ten hit. The difficult part is convincing them he's right and getting them to sign up.

programme.title: The Woman King
programme.desc: In the 1800s, Gen. Nanisca trains a group of all-female warriors to protect the African kingdom of Dahomey from a foreign enemy that's determined to destroy their way of life.

programme.title: Sleep
programme.desc: A wife must figure out how to stop her husband's sleepwalking before he harms himself or his family.

programme.title: Baby Ruby
programme.desc: The tightly scripted world of a vlogger and influencer unravels after she becomes a mother.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' British adventure has crossed over the Irish sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by his great pal and famous local chef, Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James settles right in and cooks up a fish stew for the crowds of tourists. Paul goes in search of Irish gold and finds it in the form of rapeseed at the Kane family farm in Limavady.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Nigel shows how combinations of ingredients that grow in the same place at the same time often make the ideal starting point for a dish. There is no need to write down complicated recipes as Nigel's unflustered style makes these feasts easy to remember. Nigel also visits local allotment holders and creates a tasty feast from the fruits of their labour.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Mouth-watering chocolate cookies and crispy duck with honey and figs prove that a little bit of what you fancy does you good. Then to balance the scales, Nigel makes a couple of dishes full of clean-tasting flavours, with fresh herbs, tomatoes and crunchy greens - but whether saints or sinners, every dish will taste truly divine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree fills the fridge with her kids' favourite foods while they all help out at the Merc. She prepares Paige's quiche, restorative chicken noodle soup and berry yoghurt parfaits.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree tests delicious ideas for kitchen hacks to up her breakfast game. Recipes include shortcut chocolate chip hazelnut pancakes, crazy omelettes in a bag, cheesy wafflemaker hash browns and mango marvel smoothie packs.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a tasty tikka roast chicken, a vibrant carrot and grain salad, a super-speedy crab spaghetti, and Jamie's chocolate orange shortbread.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a super-fast, stress-free sausage carbonara with a peppery punch. There's also a spicy one-pot Thai red chicken soup; a scrambled egg omelette; and steamed pudding with a speedy, sticky marmalade glaze.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Jeffrey's Faves

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's a Bake Off first - Japanese Week - and the bakers tackle steamed buns, a layered technical and a showstopper inspired by kawaii, the Japanese love of all things cute.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary has always adored entertaining and pulls out some of her finest classics that will leave any guest begging for the next invitation, be it a romantic meal for two or hosting a big meal for friends and family. From her divine Dover sole cooked to perfection, a genius way to make beetroot gravadlax and a sticky chicken recipe that will have them knocking down the doors, to an ambitious crispy roast duck and a simple but exquisite raspberry mousse.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James heads to Brooklyn, where a local baker shows him around, before making some Whoopie Pies. He then meets a larger than life Brooklyn baking master.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James travels to Harlem to find a bakery in the heart of the community famous for its carrot cake, before moving on to a blueberry farm.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: Chef James Martin searches for more great American cakes. He visits that American institution, the diner, for a traditional pancake breakfast.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James is invited into the home of Connecticut local, who makes him a Jewish holiday honey cake, before lending a hand at a local cooking class.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: Crete has a rich and complex culinary history. In the ancient capital of Heraklion, Yotam makes a variation of the Cretan meze dakos, a marriage of tomatoes, crumbled feta, olives and oregano.

programme.title: Forever Summer With Nigella
programme.desc: Nigella's in the pink in this edition, using a classic strawberry ice cream to rework the traditional strawberries and cream dish. Other recipes given the Lawson makeover are spiced pinked soup, watermelon, feta and black olive salad, and pink seared lamb with bulgur wheat salad.

programme.title: Forever Summer With Nigella
programme.desc: Nigella travels to Whitstable and makes ricotta hotcakes with strawberries, golden coconut and turmeric-infused fish curry, a white lady cocktail, Vietnamese rice paper rolls and chocolate and passion fruit mousse.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy follows the regulars to an El Salvadoran cafe in Santa Fe, a barbecue haunt in Salt Lake City and a creole spot in Mississippi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy uncovers history with a 60-year-old coffee shop in San Diego, a family restaurant in Chicago and a 50-year-old diner in Atlanta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy takes Route 66 to sample southwest style in Arizona, a gas station turned diner in New Mexico, and top fried chicken in Chicago.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tries barbecues with mesquite classics in Texas, a train carriage restaurant in California, and regional styles in Connecticut.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's cross-country culinary quest brings him to Portsmouth, New Hampshire. Adam's first stop is Gilley's, which has been serving up delicious burgers for nearly a century.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's tasty travels take him on a road trip across the Main Street of America, Route 66. Adam's first stop is in St Louis, Missouri, to try some famous frozen custards.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Things quickly get out-of-hand when Buddy fills an order for an ice-fishing cake. Meanwhile, an important client requests a naked cake and Buddy is not impressed.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The juices get flowing when Buddy and the team go for a consultation at a butchers and take a slab of inspiration home for lunch.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina assembles a gorgeous seasonal brunch. On the menu are morning glory muffins, a fresh fruit platter, shakshuka with feta, and a spicy bloody Mary.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Food campaigner Henry Dimbleby joins Prue to explain how he convinced Michelin-starred chefs to work in school kitchens as her husband John goes fishing for supper.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes Jamie's Fried Chicken, and a frugal noodle dish. There's also a sweet and spicy beef tagine, and tips on cheese.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shows how having a couple of meat-free meals each week is an easy way to slash costs in the kitchen with his show-stopping aubergine dahl, with Indian rice and homemade chapatis.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: This episode is all about cakes with plenty of twists on traditional recipes, including chocolate cola cake, boiled orange and brazil nut cake, honey and hazelnut cake, and Granny's dripping cake.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares trout salad and hot tea-smoked trout with celeriac remoulade. He also tries his hand at fly fishing for trout on the River Test in Hampshire and smokes a trout at John Robinson's butcher's.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares penne carbonara, smoked haddock risotto and tiramisu and talks about classic Italian ingredients with regular supplier.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James Martin prepares chilli mushroom, pasta with cepes sauce and coffee mushroom dessert. He goes looking for wild mushrooms in the New Forest and provides a guide to the different types available in Britain.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel Roux starts his journey at his family's riverside restaurant and Heston Blumenthal's pub. Plus he cooks up lobster ravioli and venison pies.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: In Henley, Michel visits a local butcher, farmer's market and a chocolate fuelled café. He also cooks a duck burger, cheese and ham pie, and lemon tarts.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel goes fishing with an old friend and makes an alfresco fish dish by the river. Plus, he makes watercress soup, a winter salad, and roast apples.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel arrives in London, where he goes mud larking and visits a 500-year-old pub. He cooks pan roast chicken, mussel stew and pear and plum crumble.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: In Southend, Michel meets a family of fishmongers and chefs keeping it local. Plus, he makes scallop curry, prawn toast, and fried custard beignets.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Food campaigner Henry Dimbleby joins Prue to explain how he convinced Michelin-starred chefs to work in school kitchens as her husband John goes fishing for supper.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how people and resources became absorbed in the war efforts by occupying forces during WWII.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.desc: Director Frank Capra's World War II "Why We Fight" series ends with this portrait of the people.

programme.desc: Examining the Doolittle Raid on Tokyo from the perspectives of the children of the Doolittle Raiders and Chinese villagers who aided in their rescue.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Revealing the strange Slavic story of Ivan and Koschei the Deathless, the origins of King Arthur and the wizard Merlin, the journey of Odysseus as he resists the beautiful song of the Sirens, and finally Sigurd and his battle with the dragon Fafnir.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Discovering the tragic Greek myth of Actaeon, who stumbled upon the goddess Artemis, along with the Celtic Otherworld, an idyllic place of magic and beauty, and hunting for the Kraken before venturing into the woods of the Brothers Grimm.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette embark upon an epic search through the River Ouse, which winds its way through North Yorkshire, in a bid to find evidence that it was once home to Viking life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019's most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the city's story.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Myths and legends affect the consolidation of the rules of society, as well as the punishments that result from the violation of the rules.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters
programme.desc: Society has always has had a fascination with the dead and the myths and legends associated with the afterlife reflect what different cultures value most in life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts follows the excavation of Iron Age Britain's most spectacular grave. A team of archaeologists in East Yorkshire have uncovered the remains of only the third upright chariot burial ever found in Britain.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019's most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the River Lochy in search for evidence of the clashing Scottish clans embroiled in the 17th century civil war.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the cathedral city of Canterbury in Kent, where they are granted special permission to undertake the first official search of this section of the River Stour.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In York, Professor Alice Roberts takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UK's greatest archaeological finds, and gets her hands on Viking poo.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britain's most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy is back in Cornwall searching for legends, meeting a real-life mermaid and fishing for Cornwall's infamous sea serpent Morgawr.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chief's citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henry's vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Following the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171, Henry II himself arrives to takes control of Ireland, and grants land there to settlers from Britain. Now, archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives.

programme.title: Clash of Ancient Warriors
programme.desc: Using modern science to investigate mysteries surrounding some of ancient history's most famous battles. Each episode retraces a team's investigation and attempts to reconstruct an hour-by-hour account of a legendary skirmish.

programme.title: Clash of Ancient Warriors
programme.desc: The Persian king Darius the Great sends an expeditionary force to Greece in retaliation for Athens' support of a revolt among the Greek cities of Ionia. When the Persian army lands, it is met by the Athenian army.

programme.title: Walking Through History

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a recently married man who with his bride buys a three-bedroom house in Streatham. He jokes that although she is an interior designer he is worried because she has expensive taste.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy look around properties sold under the hammer, including a well-cared-for house in Cornwall which comes with a stunning garden that takes Martin's breath away.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy travel between the North and South visiting two semis and a bungalow all sold under the hammer. All three properties need major renovation work but will any of the developers manage to get the job done according to plan?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three sisters join forces to buy a three bedroom flat in Streatham, London, as an investment property, but will there be any fallings out before the project is done? Also, properties in Derbyshire and County Durham with different renovation budgets.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An eight bedroom house, a semi detached and an end of terrace are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander meet the developers all keen to make some money on their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple fall in love with a converted stable block in North Yorkshire, but will it be enduring love after all the work is done? Also, projects get under way in Stoke and Tunbridge Wells.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks around a tired-looking bungalow in County Durham bought by a couple with three labradors and meets a new property developer who is working on a terraced house in Wales, while Lucy follows the progress of a flat in central London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A ground-floor flat, an end-terrace house and a bungalow, all in need of modernisation, are sold under the hammer, but will the new owners make any money on their investments? Martin and Lucy have a look around and check up on progress.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a substantial Kent property divided into flats, a three-bedroom house, small workshops in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in a leafy Surrey town.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Cornish farmhouse with stunning coastal views, a garden flat in Essex and a grade two listed Georgian house in Kent all go under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners and hear their renovation plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy meet a father-and-son team with high-end plans for a Surrey bungalow, two friends who plan to renovate a one-bedroom flat in Gateshead and a man with lofty ambitions to convert an Edwardian water tower into a home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A piece of land in Kent, a terraced house in South Wales and a flat with a quirky layout in the borders of Scotland all go under the Hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners but will any of them manage to make money on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A piece of land, a flat and a semi are developed on today's show with very differing times, ranging from one day to four years. Martin and Lucy travel between Kent and Durham to check out the projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple who buy two bungalows on a plot in Devon create a modern home making the most of the stunning views, while a terraced house in Stoke and a flat near Glasgow also get refurbished - but do any of the developers stand to make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A terraced house in Stoke, a huge office in Ashford and a listed Victorian house in Plymouth are all renovated over very different timescales from a few months to four years.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a family affair today when Lucy and Martin follow development projects run by parents and their children, but at the end of it, will it still be a case of happy families?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two studio flats - one in London, the other in Scotland - go under the hammer for very different prices. Martin meets a former London football coach and his model son who take on a semi in Stoke - but will they score an own goal?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin falls in love with the seaside views of a Devon cliffside house, while Lucy squeezes into a bijou London pad as well as doing the maths - will the profit add up for five Ramsgate flats?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin meets a man who buys a house in Wales to give his mother-in-law, who lives next door, peace of mind after it has stood empty and neglected for 12 years. Two other properties in Derbyshire and Kent also get renovated.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: In Folkestone, Kent, Lucy finds a big renovation project looking for just the right purchaser. Martin looks around a first-class opportunity in the form of a disused post office and visits a couple who are starting their lives afresh with a mid-terrace house in Staffordshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy finds hidden depths in a Ramsgate listed building while Martin meets a buyer who's more than a little surprised by his auction purchase in Dunmurry, Northern Ireland. In Earls Court, London, we find a little flat with big potential.

This week, she meets a Bulldog who has one of the worst breathing problems the vets have ever encountered, helps a Collie Cross get over a past trauma, and goes out on the streets of East London to meet the vets treating the dogs of people experiencing homelessness.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The boys have arrived in Brisbane and have swapped the RV for electric scooters as they explore the city. The pair head to the local racetrack, where they go head-to-head in a sport known to the locals as 'the trots'. They end the day with a group of people who gather every week to immerse themselves in medieval fantasy role-play. The next day, Bradley and Barney hit the road and head to the city of Rockhampton, where they meet a man who went viral thanks to a very unexpected talent.

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: On the rugged shores of Ballintoy, famed as the Iron Islands in Game of Thrones, Dermot journeys into the Atlantic with local fisherman Gordy, in search of the thrill of the catch along one of Ireland's most dramatic coastlines. Back ashore, Gordy and Dermot cook up their catch, transporting Dermot back to his childhood. Dermot travels to Bushmills, and steps into the kitchen of acclaimed restaurant Tartine.

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh at Manor Farm are actor Neil Stuke and singing sensation Marisha Wallace. Plus, florist Jonathan Moseley is celebrating the delights of the Chelsea Flower Show, some birds of prey take flight at the farm, and the wines often derided that are now making a comeback.

programme.title: Ainsley's Fantastic Flavours
programme.desc: Taking inspiration from Mother Nature, Ainsley gets green in the kitchen. Making use of wonderful seasonal ingredients picked at their best, he transforms them into dishes bursting with colour, flavour, and just a little touch of magic! Ainsley knocks up a plate of tempting celeriac steaks and shows us a cheeky twist on orange cake. Singer Tony Hadley visits the kitchen to rustle up a comforting pasta dish, and in Malta Ainsley explores a local potato farm in the sun.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: On the sweeping sands of Benone Strand, Dermot rolls up his trousers and wades into the sea to harvest water for salt-making. Guided by Clare from Mussenden Salt, he learns the traditional process of creating sea salt over an open fire right on the beach. Embracing ancient Irish traditions, they host a clan-style cookout, roasting meat, cheese, and vegetables over open flames as previous generations once did.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live LIVE
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani presents live racing from Haydock, Goodwood, York and The Curragh. Haydock stages the feature flat card in the UK with two sprints taking centre stage - the Temple Stakes for the older horses and the Sandy Lane Stakes for the three-year-olds, whilst at The Curragh it's the first classic of the season as the colts line up for the Irish 2,000 Guineas. Analysis comes from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, with commentary from Stewart Machin.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The team finally break down the wall of silence maintained by their four murder suspects and uncover the shockingly banal truth behind the killings. programme.desc: Set in the not-too-distant future when aliens, labelled `newcomers' by humans, have settled on Earth but have yet to integrate fully into society due to their hosts' prejudice. When the partner of a tough LA cop is murdered by an alien, he reluctantly agrees to work with a `newcomer' in a bid to track down the killer, but the unlikely pair soon uncover evidence of a plot which threatens to destroy both their species.

programme.title: The Hard Way
programme.desc: A pampered movie star is desperate to land the lead role in a new police thriller. Unfortunately for him, Mel Gibson is also up for the part. So the actor, ever professional, jumps in at the deep end by teaming up with a tough lawman in an effort to discover what makes him tick, but he gets more than he bargained for when the cop takes him on the trail of a deadly sniper.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Surrey and Middlesex, where Henry restores a pair of racing go-karts and Simon repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In this episode Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Holland Hall Hotel in Upholland, Lancashire run by colleagues Genice Hall and Cathy Hudson. There's a battle of the breakfasts, and feedback that cuts deep.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Railway Inn near Hull, run by couple Lydia Perkins and Luke Mainprize. The guests find little to complain about, but the pressure is on for Luke's breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to The Driftwood bed and breakfast in Weymouth, run by Sarah and Pete Healey. On arrival Cathy and Genice find the place completely ship shape and Lygia spies the host's disco balls.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Bull Hotel in Wrotham, Kent, where owner Lygia Fontanella-Deadman and breakfast chef Mary Murray pride themselves on a luxury stay, but emotions run high.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the final day of the competition and the owners are meeting for the last time to find out what they have been paid. There is some surprising feedback before the winner is announced.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This brand new competition comes from Llanelli, south Wales, where first to host is house proud council manager Charlotte, who hopes to win over her guests by injecting a bit of her own personality into each course.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day two in Llanelli and the turn of electrician and bachelor Steve, whose menu includes lasagne, vegetable soup and some fruitful homemade wine. In a lively evening of dining, Meic 'bares all' with a revelation which shocks Christine.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Christine is third to host in Llanelli and plans to impress her guests with her menu of family favourites and a performance from her number one rock band. Her night starts with a shocking discovery when Charlotte and Meic stumble across Christine's messy office.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the fourth day of the competition in Llanelli and the turn of retired French teacher Meic to host. Meic plans a revelation for his guests as well as an authentic French menu that includes poulet Basquaise.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in Llanelli and the turn of teaching assistant Ainsley to host. Ainsley wants to educate her guests and hopefully win the competition by treating them all to an authentic Russian themed dinner party.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Huddersfield where John Whitworth hopes the unique style of The Edge Accommodation has enough personality to win over his guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Darwin Lake Holiday Village in Matlock, managed by best friends Sally McAnulty and Teresa Shirley, who take pride on their exclusive village feel.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit the group head to Sands Resort Hotel and Spa in Newquay, where father and son team Nicholas and Tom Malcolm pride themselves on the vast facilities they have on offer.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Oak Tree Lane Yurt Site in Bratton Clovelly, Devon, where owner Phil Gilbert is taking things back to basics. On arrival, the guests are wowed by the Mongolian yurts.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Pyramid Tool Kit, The Killer Foot Machine And The Lost Head
programme.desc: Strange objects found hidden inside the Great Pyramid at Giza. A polished wooden device designed to bombard the human body with potentially lethal X-Rays. And a controversial terracotta sculpture.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Ex-U.S. Intelligence officer Grusch tells the Oversight Committee about an alien craft in U.S. secret facilities.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Claims of a demonic truck driver, disembodied voices, nightmarish skinwalkers and a time loop are reported in Canyonlands National Park.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: In the white ice of Antarctica, satellites discover a mysterious trail of sinister black objects, and when experts discover that it's moving, they use cutting-edge tech to investigate who or what is behind this strange phenomena.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice
programme.desc: Discover more incredible history buried frozen in the ice, from a body buried on the Himalayas' Killer Mountain to the remains of a Woolly Mammoth.

programme.title: Alien Files Reopened
programme.desc: On a September morning in 1994, students at a Zimbabwe school witness a silver craft land in a nearby field; numerous adults corroborate the UFO sighting, but the children are silenced; many maintain their account of the incident is true.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: A hitchhiker spots a pattern of lights and is terrified when they seem to attack him. A sighting in France becomes one of the most investigated cases in UFO history.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Strange light patterns are seen across multiple counties in Texas. In France, after an intense encounter, a man's previous knee injury is completely healed.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: The tourist-attracting waters of Crater Lake National Park may also be the epicenter of dark and sinister forces from Native American lore that lure visitors to their deaths.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Claims of a demonic truck driver, disembodied voices, nightmarish skinwalkers and a time loop are reported in Canyonlands National Park.

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: Several UFOs suddenly appear above Swedish power plants and other places. Some believe that the country will soon be the target of an attack. A soldier claims to have encountered a UFO years ago and receives compensation from the US military.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: When a sinister cave is discovered in a remote corner of Iceland, experts uncover a secret underworld tunneling to unknown depths, and using cutting-edge tech, they investigate if this strange place could hold secrets of alien worlds.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: In the white ice of Antarctica, satellites discover a mysterious trail of sinister black objects, and when experts discover that it's moving, they use cutting-edge tech to investigate who or what is behind this strange phenomena.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice
programme.desc: Discover more incredible history buried frozen in the ice, from a body buried on the Himalayas' Killer Mountain to the remains of a Woolly Mammoth.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice
programme.desc: See more incredible stories frozen in time, from the mummified remains of three Inca children in the Andes to members of the ancient Yupik people in Alaska.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Some places in the world record more UAP sightings than the rest, attracting people hoping to glimpse a flying saucer.

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: Several UFOs suddenly appear above Swedish power plants and other places. Some believe that the country will soon be the target of an attack. A soldier claims to have encountered a UFO years ago and receives compensation from the US military.

programme.title: Alien Files Reopened
programme.desc: Physicist Paul Bennewitz is convinced he has located a top secret human and alien underground lab in New Mexico. The US Military thinks Bennewitz has come too close to top secret information and send Air Force investigators to feed him lies.

programme.title: Close Encounters Down Under
programme.desc: Queensland's Tropical Coast: Delve into the incredible collection of UFO stories of first-hand witnesses, from flying saucers to strange encounters in sugar cane fields.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: From mid-air abductions to near-disasters caused by unseen enemies, experts examine if we are alone in the skies over Earth.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the mounting evidence that suggests UFO waves are able to penetrate the world's most closely guarded airspace at will.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Meeting people who experienced alien life up close and examining three types of close encounters: sightings, fleeting interactions and abductions.

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: A UFO is caught on camera by a military helicopter on the Mexican border, an English woman is filmed disappearing from her bed at night, and NASA investigates UFOs after years of denial by the US authorities.

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Mysterious industrial waste is discovered in an uninhabited part of Greenland. Meanwhile, strange mounds are found deep within the Arctic Circle.

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A group of hikers trekking across the Italian Alps stumble upon abandoned military infrastructure. What purpose did these camouflaged buildings serve?

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Graves dating back some 34,000 years are discovered in Russia, and a team attempts to locate a British aeroplane that went missing in Iceland during the Second World War.

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Off the coast of Antarctica, a volcanic island with a mysterious past is explored. The mummified corpse of a teenage girl is found in the Peruvian Andes. And a shipwreck is found near the Franz Josef archipelago in Russia.

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Some places in the world record more UAP sightings than the rest, attracting people hoping to glimpse a flying saucer.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice
programme.desc: See more incredible stories frozen in time, from the mummified remains of three Inca children in the Andes to members of the ancient Yupik people in Alaska.

programme.title: Lords of War
programme.desc: In Cape Coral, Florida, Sean discovers one of his favourite weapons: an 18th-century Queen Anne flintlock pistol that was also used as a sword!

programme.desc: A look at how some foods are made in food factories including fat-free Greek yogurt, butter cream, and Swiss roll cakes.

programme.title: Food Factory
programme.desc: A look at how some foods are made in food factories including cabbage rolls, sweet and salty popcorn, and potato skins chips.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Keeper Tales
programme.sub-title: If You Give a Giraffe a Pedicure
programme.desc: It's springtime at the Columbus Zoo, which means annual giraffe checkups with extensive hoof care and more.

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Keeper Tales
programme.sub-title: The Devil Went Down to Columbus
programme.desc: Out at the Wilds, vets tag newborn Pere David deers. Plus, keepers at the markhor exhibit get ready to welcome a batch of tiny babies.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Lost Ships of WW2
programme.desc: The Petrel team searches for the American aircraft carrier USS Hornet. The Doolittle Raid was launched off her deck to avenge Pearl Harbor. But six months later, the ship's life was cut short when the Hornet sailed into Japanese ships.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation Special Report
programme.desc: Confused, flying blind and lost in the dark, three crews lose site of the horizon and fall into the same deadly trap.

programme.title: Inside the CIA: Secrets & Spies
programme.desc: The unforeseen arrest of an American in Pakistan threatens one of the most secretive missions in CIA history.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists investigate one of the great mysteries of ancient Egypt: the mummy. Teams excavate buried tombs and make shocking new discoveries.

programme.title: World War II: Secrets From Above
programme.desc: The longest campaign of the war pits the deadly stealth of the Nazi U-boats against the code-breaking genius and long-range bombers of the Allies.

programme.title: Plane Crash Recreated
programme.desc: In July 1989, United Airlines flight 232 was forced to make an emergency landing after an explosion. With only two working engines, the crew on board faced the task of landing with a total loss of control.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Moments after taking off from a California airport, an American cargo plane needs to make an emergency landing. The pilots manage to bring their DC-8 to within sight of the runway, but the crippled plane crashes into an automobile salvage yard one mile east of the airport.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: America's War
programme.desc: A closer look at the secret preparation and torpedo technology that was behind Japan's devastating surprise air attack on America's Pacific Fleet in 1941.

programme.title: Seconds From Disaster
programme.desc: In the worst single-aircraft accident in aviation history, a Boeing 747 crashed into a mountain in 1985, killing 520 people. How did it happen?

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered
programme.desc: Toronto Airport takes safety seriously, as challenges impact operations.

programme.title: Hitler's Last Stand
programme.desc: A US Parachute Infantry Regiment rushes to protect a critical bridge from a Nazi battle group during the opening days of the Battle of the Bulge.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by a cascade of conflicting error messages, Aeroperú Flight 603 plunges into the Pacific, instantly killing everyone on board; a navy expedition to the sea floor successfully retrieves the plane's black boxes.

programme.title: Titanic: Case Closed
programme.desc: Join Tim Maltin as he explores unheard evidence and overlooked details to reveal the main reason why the Titanic sank - a simple trick of light.

programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: Examine construction fails in flash-flood mayhem, cable-car catastrophes, car park fires, a train station collapse, bike path boondoggles, and a faulty bridge.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: A turboprop narrowly averts disaster after a lightning strike over the North Sea. Investigators uncover an unusual feature in the plane that plays a key role in this calamity.

programme.desc: Jay's salvaging crew seeks riches in an old abandoned bank that was built to hold the spectacular fortunes of America's big oil barons.
