programme.title: There's Something in the Barn
programme.desc: Bill moves his American wife Carol and children Nora and Lucas to Norway where he's inherited a family estate. There, Lucas discovers a mischievous barn elf from ancient folklore living in the barn who elf plots to get rid of the American intruders.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Battleship
programme.desc: A fleet of ships is forced to do battle with an armada of unknown origins in order to discover and thwart their destructive goals.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Innerspace
programme.desc: A scientifically miniaturised marine unexpectedly finds himself floating around the body of a hypochondriac, and trying to outwit saboteurs who want the device that shrank him. Attempts by his colleagues to retrieve the little man - protected inside a tiny submersible - are hampered by the would-be thieves.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Lost Boys
programme.desc: Fang-in-cheek tale about a single-parent family who move to the coastal town of Santa Clara where the local juvenile delinquents are actually a pack of vampires.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Craft
programme.desc: A new girl at an LA high school finds it difficult to make friends, and teams up with three fellow outcasts known as the Bitches of Eastwick. However, her telekinetic powers turn the supernatural games in which the three have been dabbling, into something much more sinister.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
programme.desc: Plagued by a violent and recurring nightmare, a college student heads home to track down the one person who might be able to break the cycle of death and save her family from the grisly demise that inevitably awaits them all.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: M3GAN 2.0
programme.desc: A military-grade robotic weapon known as Amelia becomes increasingly self-aware and dangerous to the human race. Hoping to stop it, Gemma, an advocate for oversight of AI, decides to resurrect M3GAN, making it faster, stronger, and more lethal.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Blade II

programme.title: Psycho
programme.desc: Classic chiller about the grisly goings-on in a remote motel run by a timid and decidedly odd young man and his domineering mother. The film totally changed Hollywood's perception of the horror genre and broke many of the movie industry's taboos.

programme.title: Ocean's Twelve
programme.desc: Casino mobster Benedict knows who stole his money and he wants it back. Danny and his crew of thieves have just two weeks to steal $190m, or their lives will be forfeit, but who is the mystery thief competing for the loot? This time Benedict's ex-girlfriend is on their side, even an integral part of the plan.

programme.title: The Day of the Jackal
programme.desc: An international hit man and quick-change artist is hired by a secret organisation to assassinate Charles de Gaulle.

programme.title: The Bourne Identity
programme.desc: An amnesiac is rescued at sea by the crew of an Italian fishing boat. Nearly dead, he carries nothing but the bullets in his back and the bank account number embedded in his hip. Although completely without identity or background, he possesses an array of extraordinary talents in fighting, linguistics and self-defence that speak of a dangerous past.

programme.title: The Equalizer
programme.desc: A former commando who had faked his own death comes out of retirement to rescue a young girl from violent Russian mobsters.

programme.title: The Equalizer 2
programme.desc: Robert McCall's mysterious past cuts especially close to home when thugs kill Susan Plummer, his best friend and former colleague. Now out for revenge, McCall must take on a crew of highly trained assassins who will stop at nothing to destroy him.

programme.title: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: A lieutenant general and a colonel face political opposition after ordering a drone missile strike to take out a group of suicide bombers in Nairobi, Kenya.

programme.title: The Bourne Supremacy
programme.desc: Life never becomes easy for ex-CIA agent Jason Bourne, who is forced out of hiding, and must return to Europe to find out exactly what happened in his past. It's not easy, as he's being framed for murder and his old employers are leading the manhunt.

programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins star as Jamie and Fran, two misfits and best friends who always have each other's back. Though their assignments constantly put their own lives at stake, the duo's endless bickering and inane antics mean they must carry out hits without being derailed by their own incompetence. Their friendship is put to the test when Fran questions whether this is the life she wants for herself.

programme.desc: Wildlife expert and safari guide Jean Du Plessis travels to Mahale Mountains National Park in the hopes of spending time with the wild chimpanzees that live in the forests of this remote and protected corner of Tanzania.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: Winter is the ultimate challenge. As temperatures plunge across much of America, some animals escape to milder climes of the South, while others stay put and tough out the brutal cold and blizzards that blanket the North.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Track scientists who have arrived in the Kalahari to test a hypothesis: that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the region's species. Observe as they monitor behaviour, decipher data, and deliver fascinating conclusions.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: In India, Ravi the baby lion-tailed macaque faces life in a shrinking habitat and encroaching human world. Luckily he has a caring mum, a protective dad, a playful sister, and numerous, cousins, aunts and uncles all watching over him.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick is in California as he searches for answers to why underwater are forests in sharp decline, and why otters might be the solution.

programme.title: Nomads of the Serengeti
programme.desc: Wildlife expert and safari guide Jean Du Plessis travels to Mahale Mountains National Park in the hopes of spending time with the wild chimpanzees that live in the forests of this remote and protected corner of Tanzania.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The team prepare to release two beautiful and elusive spotted cats called servals. The plan goes awry when one escapes into the dangerous city of Lilongwe. Meanwhile, it's all hands on deck when a baby monkey arrives in a critical condition.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Following the team as they triage patients from a hoarding case, then free a tangled-up ibis with rescue officers Jo and Chantelle, checking on a baby koala who took a swan dive and playing the waiting game with a deadly eastern brown snake.

programme.title: Nomads of the Serengeti
programme.desc: Wildlife expert and safari guide Jean Du Plessis travels to Mahale Mountains National Park in the hopes of spending time with the wild chimpanzees that live in the forests of this remote and protected corner of Tanzania.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick is in California as he searches for answers to why underwater are forests in sharp decline, and why otters might be the solution.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: This is the incredible story of a tree that has been around for eight hundred years - that can withstand the harshest conditions - and that is more loved by insects, birds and animals than any other - the enormous Baobab. Rooted on the savannah of Malilangwe Game Reserve in southern Zimbabwe.

programme.title: Great Parks of Africa
programme.desc: The Addo Elephant Park was founded in 1931 and is 1,640km2. It hosts more than 450 elephants and other mammals and was created in response to the elephant population falling as low as eleven.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The team prepare to release two beautiful and elusive spotted cats called servals. The plan goes awry when one escapes into the dangerous city of Lilongwe. Meanwhile, it's all hands on deck when a baby monkey arrives in a critical condition.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Following the team as they triage patients from a hoarding case, then free a tangled-up ibis with rescue officers Jo and Chantelle, checking on a baby koala who took a swan dive and playing the waiting game with a deadly eastern brown snake.

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: Winter is the ultimate challenge. As temperatures plunge across much of America, some animals escape to milder climes of the South, while others stay put and tough out the brutal cold and blizzards that blanket the North.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Track scientists who have arrived in the Kalahari to test a hypothesis: that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the region's species. Observe as they monitor behaviour, decipher data, and deliver fascinating conclusions.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Discover why it takes a village to raise lion cubs in Kenya, sniff out the mating strategy of fragrant lemurs in Madagascar, cut a rug at a honeybee dance party, and listen up as a forked-tailed drongo tricks other birds out of their dinner.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Marvel at a toothy fish that helps sharks maintain their pearly whites, discover the signature calls of dolphin families, examine a capuchin monkey's nut-cracking stone tools, get the warm fuzzies when giraffes snuggle, and meet a meerkat mobster.

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: Meeting nature's most wanted to see why some species don't get the respect they deserve, including the great white shark's electrical sensors, the razor-sharp limbs of the dead leaf mantis, and the zombie-creating powers of cordyceps fungi.

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: Explore the partnership between clownfish and anemones and review the quid-pro-poo agreements of dung beetles and pitcher plants.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Follows a group of professional snake catchers on Queensland's Sunshine Coast as they go head-to-head with some of the planet's deadliest snakes.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Follows a group of professional snake catchers on Queensland's Sunshine Coast as they go head-to-head with some of the planet's deadliest snakes.

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band -- to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: Near the Snowy Mountains of southeast Australia is Wombat Wood, a wooded area dotted with the burrows of wombats.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: The breeding season brings out wombats' energy, aggression and playfulness.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough explores the volcanic islands and biological wonderland of Galapagos in this three-part documentary. Here, he charts the archipelago's explosive rise from the ocean millions of years ago, and takes a look at its unique wildlife.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: David Attenborough stumbles upon species found nowhere else in the world that show how life has to quickly adapt to the islands' ever-changing landscapes.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the unusual species that inhabit the unique islands of Galapagos, including giant tortoise Lonesome George.

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: Behind the facade of Australia's dramatic scenery is a motley crew of silent assassins, con artists, gang members and brutal brawlers, vying for supremacy amidst the harsh, dry and desolate Australian desert.

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: The lionesses from the Hollywood pride are in grave trouble, with intruders occupying their prime hunting grounds and the four dangerous Nomad lions blocking their path home.

programme.title: Boss Shark
programme.desc: The ultimate battle for dominance as scientists try to pick out the alpha male in a pack of 100-plus great white sharks off the New Zealand coast.

programme.title: Boss Shark: Battle of the Sexes
programme.desc: Witness a shark competition unlike any other, where great whites go head-to-head in jaw-dropping tests of strength. Risk it all with shark specialist Dr. Riley Elliott as he tries to answer an age-old question: Which sex reigns supreme?

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: Behind the facade of Australia's dramatic scenery is a motley crew of silent assassins, con artists, gang members and brutal brawlers, vying for supremacy amidst the harsh, dry and desolate Australian desert.

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: The lionesses from the Hollywood pride are in grave trouble, with intruders occupying their prime hunting grounds and the four dangerous Nomad lions blocking their path home.

programme.title: Pridelands: Wilderness Reborn
programme.desc: An Australian family go on an adventure to restore an old hunting ranch in South Africa. Their goal is to rebuild the overgrown habitat, create an eco-tourism ranch, and allow the natural wildlife, including predators, to move back in.

programme.title: Osprey
programme.desc: Osprey.

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: Deep in the South Caribbean Sea, explore the protected waters of Bonaire National Marine Park, home to thriving reefs, endangered sea turtles, sprawling mangroves, and a plethora of marine life.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: University sweethearts Kirstie and Tom have recently bought a new build house in Daventry. In July 2014, Alan discovered his neighbour's house was on fire and immediately went to the rescue. The two families had been close friends.

programme.desc: A ruthless German bounty hunter plots to free a war criminal who knows the whereabouts of a shipment of wartime gold. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS investigate the murder of Max Klein, an East German immigrant whose mysterious last words translated as `blue flower'. As the team piece together Max's remarkable story, Pullman attempts to gain the trust of his estranged daughter Mia.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: A documentary series that gives a historical account of the events of World War II, from its roots in the 1920s to the aftermath and the lives it profoundly influenced.

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Kirsten O'Brien takes a 15-mile walk along the River Tees near Barnard Castle, exploring the history of Upper Teesdale. Kirsten meets some of extraordinary people who call its banks home and takes a wild swim in its white waters.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the second leg of his four-part journey around the Mediterranean. Setting off from a snowy mountain ski resort in Cyprus, Simon finds an island and a capital city still deeply divided between Turkish Cypriots in the north and Greek Cypriots in the south. He then joins the UN troops who patrol the line that separates the two sides.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues his journey in Libya, a country well off the tourist trail and torn apart by revolution, insurgents from the so-called Islamic State and western air strikes. He then heads to Tunisia, a country that unlike its neighbour, has long been a tourist destination. Heading deep into the desert, the backdrop for scenes in the `Star Wars' films, Simon visits the fortress village of Chenini.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Taking a ferry across the Strait of Gibraltar, Simon's first stop is Ceuta, a Spanish exclave surrounded by Morocco. This is one of the few land borders between Africa and the European Union. Simon joins the Spanish border police who check engines and even dashboards for stowaways trying to reach Europe.

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on another epic journey around the world, uncovering extraordinary stories from the southern hemisphere. The first leg sees Simon crossing the beautiful landscapes of Namibia and Botswana, having a close encounter with hungry cheetahs, visiting the world's largest diamond mine and making a gruelling crossing of the Kalahari Desert in the search of the famous San Bushmen.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: Britain's history is recorded in it's monuments but now the landmarks are crumbling. Teams of people across the country are keeping our heritage standing, these are our landmark fixers.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A now silent site that played a pivotal role defending the U.S through the 20th century. Plus, a crumbling Italian fortress and a ghostly Ukrainian mansion.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A juggernaut of Soviet engineering which was crucial to the international space race. Deserted Scottish coastal defences and a complex that's crucial to American history.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The mysterious Cachtice Castle home to Elizabeth Bathory, said to have killed over 600 girls, and a mysterious structure on the 38th parallel.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.desc: A couple searching for their first home in Los Angeles finally found their cosy cottage. While they love their home, it's very dated, so Drew and Jonathan update its awkward layout and 1970s mirror walls to create a paradise for them to grow into.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back with him to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets the four-legged friends that call the UK's leading dog welfare charity, Dogs Trust, home. From when they are rescued, Pete plays an instrumental role in their rehabilitation process, right up until the point the dogs are safely rehomed.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Fortune smiles on a cash-strapped Mike and Alison when a TV company comes calling looking for an old house in which to film their period drama. To get the money in the bank, Alison must ensure the shoot goes without a hitch.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw a swanky dinner party to help resolve the issue. With the ghosts their only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A cash-strapped young couple inherit a grand country house, only to find it is both falling apart and teeming with the ghosts of former inhabitants.

programme.title: Deadly Waters With Captain Lee
programme.desc: After seasickness and choppy waters cause a wife to fall from her husband's fishing vessel, the race is on to save her from the waters of Lake Erie. However, a deeper dive reveals a shocking illness and hidden truth about the couple's relationship.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Doug's fiancée Stephanie won't move in until he cleans up his extreme tiki collection and his dead ex-wife's stuff; the Death Cleaners help Doug clean the clutter in his heart as well as his home so he can welcome in a new chapter.

programme.desc: Anna and Darren rush to the aid of a woman suffering from terminal cancer. Laura and Kerry are called to attend a young man who is suffering from an extremely painful abscess in his throat.

programme.desc: On Sri Lanka, the Chinese are building a huge new port and their money has helped the Sri Lankan government bring a 30-year war to a brutal end. In the Indonesian capital of Jakarta, Simon investigates the shocking trade in exotic pets.

programme.desc: Simon Reeve takes a 25,000-mile journey around the Equator. He catches malaria in Gabon and struggles on through the war-ravaged Democratic Republic of Congo, goes rafting in the source of the Nile and witnesses a bullfight in Kenya. His journey ends in the desert on the Somalia border where thousands of refugees have escaped the fighting in their homeland.

programme.title: Jagadhatri
programme.desc: Jagaddhatri juggles her crime branch duties and secret identity while running an NGO. As cases pile up, her double life begins to unravel, testing her strength and the truth she hides.

programme.title: Vasudha
programme.desc: Vasudha, a cheerful maid, joins business leader Chandrika's home. Despite differing beliefs, she seeks Chandrika's approval and struggles to fit into her elite world.

programme.title: Tum Se Tum Tak
programme.desc: Anu, a kind 20-year-old, and Aryavardhan, a resolute 45-year-old businessman, defy societal norms and family resistance, overcoming struggles to demonstrate that love transcends age or boundaries.

programme.title: Ganga Mai Ki Betiyaan
programme.desc: Ganga Mai is a resilient woman who is deserted by her husband for not giving birth to a son. Refusing to bow to societal expectations, she devotes herself to raising her three daughters with unwavering strength, grace, and affection.

programme.title: Saru
programme.desc: A kabaddi champion from Rajasthan moves to Mumbai, where she battles all odds, reunites with her family, and fights to get her mother her rightful place in the family while chasing her dreams.
